Ayah

تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى نَزَّلَ ٱلْفُرْقَانَ عَلَىٰ عَبْدِهِۦ لِيَكُونَ لِلْعَـٰلَمِينَ نَذِيرًا (۱)
Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion to His servant that he may be a warner to all the nations. (1)

Key Points

The word tabārak (تَبارك) might derive from the word barak (بَرَك), meaning stable and enduring, or it might derive from the word barakah (بَركة), meaning abundant goodness. This means that the One Who revealed the Quran is the source of immense goodness, and the revelation of the Quran itself is an example of His blessing and goodness. Indeed, what blessing is greater than the Divine Law which has guided billions of people throughout history from darknesses into light? Nuzūl (revelation), if presented in the form of anzalnā (اَنزَلنا), means sending down all at once and instantaneously. However, if it is in the form of nazzala (نَزّل), it means sending down gradually. The Quran possesses both types of revelation. Once, it was revealed instantaneously to the Prophet on the Night of Qadr (Power): «اِنّا انزَلناه فى لیلة القَدر» (239). And another time, it was revealed gradually over a period of 23 years: «نزّل الفرقان». Just as we read elsewhere in the Quran: «و قُرآناً فَرَقناه لِتَقرأه على النّاس على مُكثٍ و نزّلناه تنزیلا» (240). We revealed the Quran in portions so that you may recite it to the people gradually.

239) Qadr, 1. 240) Isra, 106.

Messages

1- The Quran originated from a blessed source. «تبارك الّذى نزّل»

2- Both the cosmic system of man (his creation, which was accomplished by the power of Allah) is worthy of praise, «فتبارك اللّه احسن الخالقین» (241), and His legislative system (the revelation of the Quran and the codification of religious laws). «تبارك الّذى نزّل»

3- The Quran was revealed gradually. «نزّل»

4- The Quran is the best means of distinguishing truth from falsehood. «الفرقان»

5- If the heavenly books and the mission of the prophets did not exist, humanity would be bewildered and lost. «الفرقان»

6- Servitude and worship are the prerequisites for receiving revelation. «نزّل الفرقان على عبده»

7- The mission of the Prophet of Islam is universal. «للعالمین»

8- The duty of the prophets is to warn and admonish. «نذیرا»

241) Mu’minun, 14.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Furqan

This Surah has seventy-seven verses and was revealed in Mecca. In the first verse of this Surah, the Quran is referred to as Al-Furqan, which means the separator of truth from falsehood. Hence, this Surah has been named "Al-Furqan." This Surah includes verses concerning the excuses of the polytheists and the response to them, the history of previous nations such as the Companions of the Rass (Aṣḥāb ar-Rass), the regret of people on the Day of Judgment, the signs of Monotheism and the greatness of Allah in nature, and a comparison between the believers and the disbelievers. However, the most important section of the verses in this Surah concerns the characteristics of ‘Ibād ar-Raḥmān (عباد الرّحمن), meaning the true servants of Allah, which spans from verse 63 to the end of the Surah. We hope that by reciting and reflecting upon these verses and reforming our conduct and actions based on these attributes, we may prepare the grounds for receiving Divine favors in this world and the Hereafter, and for entering the sublime Paradise.

Ayah

ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَمْ يَتَّخِذْ وَلَدًا وَلَمْ يَكُن لَّهُۥ شَرِيكٌ فِى ٱلْمُلْكِ وَخَلَقَ كُلَّ شَىْءٍ فَقَدَّرَهُۥ تَقْدِيرًا (۲)
He, to whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and who did not take any offspring, nor has He any partner in sovereignty, and He created everything and determined it in a precise measure. (2)

Key Points

The polytheists, the Jews, and the Christians, each in some way, believed that Allah has a son or a partner, and the Noble Quran has repeatedly rejected this superstitious belief, including in this very Ayah.

Messages

1- The governance and sovereignty of existence belong exclusively to Allah. «له ملك ...» (A single system, a single Will, and a single management govern existence.)

2- Allah’s governance is uniform across all of existence. «السموات والارض»

3- Allah is self-sufficient (free of need). «له ملك السموات... لم یتخذ» (The need for a son is to compensate for deficiencies, and what deficiency could He have, Whose hand holds the governance of existence?!)

4- All created things originate from a single source of power. «خلق كل شیى ء»

5- The administration (or planning) of existence is in the hands of the Creator of existence. «له ملك السموات... خلق كلّ شى ء»

6- Creation has order and measure (it is calculated). «فقدره تقدیرا»

7- The administration of existence is extremely precise. «تقدیرا»

Ayah

وَٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ ءَالِهَةً لَّا يَخْلُقُونَ شَيْـًٔا وَهُمْ يُخْلَقُونَ وَلَا يَمْلِكُونَ لِأَنفُسِهِمْ ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا وَلَا يَمْلِكُونَ مَوْتًا وَلَا حَيَوٰةً وَلَا نُشُورًا (۳)
Yet they have taken gods besides Him who create nothing and have themselves been created, and who have no control over their own harm or benefit and have no control over [their own] death, life, or resurrection. (3)

Messages

1- Belief in God is innate (fitrah), and if someone abandons the One God, he turns to false gods. «واتّخذوا من دونه الهة»

2- Speak logically (with evidence) even to the polytheists. «لایخلقون ... لایملكون» An animal brings benefit to itself and wards off harm from itself, but idols have no power over either of these two things and are less than an animal.

3- The reason for human servitude is Allah's power to solve his problems and untie his knots, while idols lack these effects. «لایملكون»

4- He who cannot ward off harm from himself and cannot attract benefit for himself will certainly not do anything for others. «لانفسهم»

5- Warding off harm takes precedence over attracting benefit. (The phrase «لایَملكون لانفسهم ضَرّاً», which is warding off harm, precedes «و لا نَفعا», which is attracting benefit.)

6- Human action must either be accompanied by a worldly goal or an otherworldly goal, and idols are ineffective in either. «و لا حیاة و لا نشورا»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوٓا۟ إِنْ هَـٰذَآ إِلَّآ إِفْكٌ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ وَأَعَانَهُۥ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ ءَاخَرُونَ ۖ فَقَدْ جَآءُو ظُلْمًا وَزُورًا (٤)
The faithless say, ‘This is nothing but a lie that he has fabricated, and other people have abetted him in it.’ Thus they have certainly come out with wrongdoing and falsehood. (4)

Key Points

The word 'ifk' (إفك) means lie, and 'zūr' (زور) means false and lying speech. [242] Throughout the entire Qur'an, only here are the words 'ẓulm' (ظلم - injustice) and 'zūr' (زور - falsehood) mentioned side by side, because the disbelievers prevented the guidance of others by slandering the Prophet and calling the Divine Book a lie.

242) Lisān al-'Arab.

Messages

1- Disbelievers justify their disbelief by calling the Divine Books lies. «إِنْ هَذَا إِلَّا إِفْكٌ افْتَرَاهُ»

2- Belittling the Book, «إِنْ هَذَا», and belittling the Prophets, «افْتَرَاهُ», is the method of the disbelievers.

3- Even the opponents have acknowledged the greatness of the Qur'an. «أَعَانَهُ عَلَيْهِ قَوْمٌ»

4- Deceiving the masses through belittlement, slander, and preventing the guidance of others is among the worst sins. «ظُلْمًا وَزُورًا»

Ayahs

وَقَالُوٓا۟ أَسَـٰطِيرُ ٱلْأَوَّلِينَ ٱكْتَتَبَهَا فَهِىَ تُمْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِ بُكْرَةً وَأَصِيلًا (٥)
They say, ‘He has taken down myths of the ancients, and they are dictated to him morning and evening.’ (5)
قُلْ أَنزَلَهُ ٱلَّذِى يَعْلَمُ ٱلسِّرَّ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ كَانَ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (٦)
Say, ‘It has been sent down by Him who knows the hidden in the heavens and the earth. Indeed He is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’ (6)

Key Points

The disbelievers objected both to the content of the Quran, claiming it lacked novelty and was merely ancient myths, "اساطیر الاوّلین" (tales of the ancients), and they questioned the person of the Prophet, claiming he had copied it, "اِكتَتَبها" (he has written it down), and they also imagined helpers for the Prophet, "تُملى علیه" (it is dictated to him). The intent behind [their claim of] writing down the myths is either that they believed people were writing for the Prophet, or they claimed that the Prophet was literate, thereby denying His illiteracy (ummiyyah). In this Ayah, it is stated: "یعلم السرّ" (He knows the secret), but Allah's knowledge of the apparent is not explicitly mentioned. Indeed, for the One who knows the secrets and hidden realities, His awareness of the manifest realities is certain.

Messages

1- Observe honesty when stating the claims of opponents. "قالوا اساطیر الاوّلین"

2- Respond to those who speak nonsense. "قل" (Say)

3- The Quran originates from the source of infinite knowledge, not from myths. "انزله الّذى یعلم" (It was sent down by the One who knows)

4- The Quran is not man-made; rather, it is sent down by the One who has knowledge of all the secrets of the universe. "انزله الّذى یعلم السرّ..." (It was sent down by the One who knows the secret...)

5- The Quran contains mysterious instructions. "انزله الّذى یعلم السرّ..."

6- Allah's knowledge regarding hidden and manifest realities is uniform. "یعلم السرّ" (He knows the secret)

7- There are secrets in the heavens that are beyond human reach. "السرّ فى السموات" (The secret in the heavens)

8- The path of repentance is open even for disbelievers who call the Quran myths and the Prophet a fabricator of lies. "انّه كان غفورا رحیما" (Indeed, He is Ever Forgiving, Most Merciful)

9- Forgiveness alone is not sufficient; grace and mercy are also necessary. "غفورا رحیما" (Forgiving, Merciful)

Ayah

وَقَالُوا۟ مَالِ هَـٰذَا ٱلرَّسُولِ يَأْكُلُ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمْشِى فِى ٱلْأَسْوَاقِ ۙ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ إِلَيْهِ مَلَكٌ فَيَكُونَ مَعَهُۥ نَذِيرًا (۷)
And they say, ‘What sort of apostle is this who eats food and walks in the marketplaces? Why has not an angel been sent down to him so as to be a warner along with him?’ (7)

Key Points

The disbelievers criticized the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله for eating food and walking around. Ayah 20 of this very Surah answers that all previous Prophets also ate food and walked in the markets.

Messages

1- Some people view perfection as a fault and deficiency. «ما لهذا الرسول» (An ordinary life and presence among the people is a perfection for a leader.)

2- Calling people to Allah does not contradict enjoying the world. «یأكل - یمشى»

3- A leader should not be separate from society. One who interacts frequently with the people can guide them better. «یمشى فى الاسواق»

4- The Prophets personally purchased what they needed. «یمشى فى الاسواق»

Ayah

أَوْ يُلْقَىٰٓ إِلَيْهِ كَنزٌ أَوْ تَكُونُ لَهُۥ جَنَّةٌ يَأْكُلُ مِنْهَا ۚ وَقَالَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ إِن تَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا رَجُلًا مَّسْحُورًا (۸)
Or, ‘[Why is not] a treasure thrown to him, or [why does] he [not] have a garden from which he may eat?’ And the wrongdoers say, ‘You are just following a bewitched man.’ (8)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the expectation of the disbelievers was that the Prophet should not have physical needs, eat food, or come among the people to fulfill His needs: «ما لِهذا الرّسول یأكل الطعام و یَمشى فى الاسواق». In this Ayah, the expectation is that the Prophet must be self-sufficient so that He does not need to be present in the market or among the people: «یُلقى الیه كَنز او تكون لَه جَنّة». They are unaware that this kind of self-sufficiency is negative, because how can a privileged and exceptional person be the leader of suffering people?

Messages

  1. Materialists look at the Prophets even from the perspective of material wealth. «یُلقى الیه كَنزٌ»

  2. Finding excuses regarding the work of the Prophets is oppression. «و قال الظالمون»

  3. The arrogant and contentious person finds an excuse at every moment, and as soon as he despairs of one, he replaces it with another. «یأكل - یَمشى - لولا اُنزل الیه - او یُلقى الیه كنز»

  4. Opponents of the Prophets sometimes called them sorcerers and sometimes bewitched (or enchanted). «رجلاً مَسحورا»

Ayah

ٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ ضَرَبُوا۟ لَكَ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلَ فَضَلُّوا۟ فَلَا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ سَبِيلًا (۹)
Look, how they coin epithets for you; so they go astray, and cannot find the way. (9)

Key Points

The meaning of the Ayah might be this: Those who hinder the guidance of the prophets by presenting undue expectations and baseless excuses will not get anywhere; meaning, they will not succeed in their goal. As the poet says: You take away your own honor and cause us trouble.

Messages

1- Observe the enemies' fault-finding (or excuse-making) carefully. «اُنظر»

2- In response to the weakening efforts of the opponents, you should also state their deviations. «ضربوا لك الامثال فضلّوا فلا یستطیعون سبیلا»

3- Inappropriate analogy and similitude are the grounds for deviation. «فضلّوا»

4- Abandoning the logic, miracles, and perfections of the Prophet and pursuing wealth, status, and excuse-making is misguidance. «فضلّوا»

Ayah

تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِىٓ إِن شَآءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ خَيْرًا مِّن ذَٰلِكَ جَنَّـٰتٍ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهَا ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ وَيَجْعَل لَّكَ قُصُورًۢا (۱٠)
Blessed is He who will grant you better than that if He wishes—gardens with streams running in them, and He will make for you palaces. (10)

Key Points

In the previous verses, it was stated that the disbelievers expected the noble Prophet of Islam to possess treasures and gardens. This verse says: If Allah wills, He would place gardens (plural) at the disposal of the Prophet instead of just one garden. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: If Allah had willed, when the prophets were commissioned, He would have opened the doors of treasures, gold mines, and lush, green gardens to them, and He would have done so. But if He had done this, the issue of testing the people would have vanished, and everyone would have gathered around the prophets out of love for wealth and comfort, and their intentions would not have been pure. (243) The word tabārak (تَبارَكَ) is either derived from bark (بَرك), meaning fixed and enduring, or from barakah (بَرَكة), meaning abundant goodness. (244)

243) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192. 244) Lisān al-ʿArab.

Messages

1- The existence of Allah is enduring and the source of all blessings (barakāt). «تَبارَكَ»

2- Asceticism (zuhd) and simplicity are appropriate for the prophets, and palace-dwelling life is far from the dignity and manner of the prophets. «إِن شَاءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ»

3- Poverty and wealth are in the hand of Allah. «إِن شَاءَ جَعَلَ لَكَ»

4- The will and desire of Allah are inevitable. «إِن شَاءَ جَعَلَ»

Ayah

بَلْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِٱلسَّاعَةِ ۖ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِمَن كَذَّبَ بِٱلسَّاعَةِ سَعِيرًا (۱۱)
Indeed, they deny the Hour, and We have prepared a Blaze for those who deny the Hour. (11)

Key Points

The word "Sa'īr" (سَعیر) means intensely blazing and burning fire.

Messages

1- The source of the disbelievers' excuses is their lack of belief in the Resurrection. «بل كذّبوا بالساعة» (The root of the disbelievers' fault-finding—asking why the Prophet does not possess treasures and gardens—is their denial of the Resurrection.)

2- Denying the Hereafter is extremely dangerous. «كذّبوا بالسّاعة... كذّب بالسّاعة»

3- Paradise and Hell have been created and prepared beforehand. «اعتدنا»

4- A major sin entails a great punishment. «كذّب - سعیرا»

Ayahs

إِذَا رَأَتْهُم مِّن مَّكَانٍۭ بَعِيدٍ سَمِعُوا۟ لَهَا تَغَيُّظًا وَزَفِيرًا (۱۲)
When it sights them from a distant place, they will hear it raging and roaring. (12)
وَإِذَآ أُلْقُوا۟ مِنْهَا مَكَانًا ضَيِّقًا مُّقَرَّنِينَ دَعَوْا۟ هُنَالِكَ ثُبُورًا (۱۳)
And when they are cast into a narrow place in it, bound together [in chains], they will pray for [their own] annihilation. (13)

Key Points

The word taghayyuz (تَغیُّظ) means the manifestation of rage and intense anger, which is sometimes accompanied by noise. Zafīr (زَفیر) refers to the sound of breathing when the chest is constricted due to intense sorrow. Muqarranīn (مُقرَّنین) means being bound with shackles and chains, and thubūr (ثُبور) means crying out "Woe!"

Hell is so vast that on the Day of Judgment, if it is asked, "Are you full?" it will say, "Is there any more?" «هل امتلأتِ فیقول هل من مَزید» [245] But despite the vastness of Hell, the transgressor will have a confined space within it. This is like a nail in a large wall; both the vastness of the wall and the narrowness of the nail's location are true. In a hadith from the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, it is stated: The inhabitants of Hell will be under pressure like a nail in a wall. [246]

Neglect of Allah in this world and the Hereafter brings severe constraints. As for this world: «یَجعل صَدره ضَیّقا حَرَجا» [247] Allah makes his chest constricted and tight, and «وَ مَن اَعرَض عن ذِكرى فانّ له مَعیشةً ضَنكا» [248] And whoever turns away from My remembrance—indeed, for him is a restricted life. And in the Hereafter: «واِذا اُلقوا منها مَكاناً ضیّقا» [249] They will be cast into a confined place in Hell.

Conversely, attention to and faith in Allah is a source of expansion both in this world: «لَفَتحنا علیهم بركاتٍ من السماء والارض» [250] We would have opened upon them blessings from the heaven and the earth. And in the Hereafter: «حتّى اذا جاؤها فُتحت ابوابها» [251] When they reach it (Paradise), its gates will be opened.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The meaning of "a distant place" (makān ba‘īd) is a distance of one year's journey. [252]

245) Qaf, 30. 246) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 247) Al-An‘ām, 125. 248) Tā Hā, 124. 249) Al-Furqān, 13. 250) Al-A‘rāf, 96. 251) Az-Zumar, 73. 252) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- Hell possesses a type of perception and feeling. «رأتهم» (It sees them)

2- The Resurrection is physical. «من مكان بعید» (from a distant place)

3- The inhabitants of Hell will be bound with shackles and chains. «مقرنین»

4- The inhabitants of Hell are thrown into Hell with humiliation. «اُلقوا» (They were cast) is used, not «اُدخلوا» (They were entered).

5- The wailing and cries of the inhabitants of Hell will be heard from afar. «دَعَوا هُنالك ثبورا» (Hunālika (هُنالك) is used for a distant location.)

Ayahs

لَّا تَدْعُوا۟ ٱلْيَوْمَ ثُبُورًا وَٰحِدًا وَٱدْعُوا۟ ثُبُورًا كَثِيرًا (۱٤)
[They will be told:] ‘Do not pray for a single annihilation today, but pray for many annihilations!’ (14)
قُلْ أَذَٰلِكَ خَيْرٌ أَمْ جَنَّةُ ٱلْخُلْدِ ٱلَّتِى وُعِدَ ٱلْمُتَّقُونَ ۚ كَانَتْ لَهُمْ جَزَآءً وَمَصِيرًا (۱٥)
Say, ‘Is that better, or the everlasting paradise promised to the Godwary, which will be their reward and destination?’ (15)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- On the Day of Resurrection, there will be no helper (or rescuer) for the disbelievers. «وادعوا ثبوراً كثیرا»

2- Paradise is eternal. «جنّة الخلد»

3- The promise of Paradise is certain. «جنّة الخلد الّتى وعد المتّقون»

Ayah

لَّهُمْ فِيهَا مَا يَشَآءُونَ خَـٰلِدِينَ ۚ كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ وَعْدًا مَّسْـُٔولًا (۱٦)
There they will have whatever they wish, abiding [forever]—a promise [much] besought, [binding] on your Lord. (16)

Key Points

The desires of the people of Paradise are, like the desires of Allah, within the framework of wisdom. Meaning, just as "یفعل ما یشاء" (He does what He wills) from Allah is in the form of wise action, "لهم فیها ما یشاؤن" (They shall have therein what they desire) from the believers is also in the form of a wise request. It is not the case that the believers would wish for the people of the Fire to be released, or request to attain the ranks of the Prophets, because such requests are not wise.

Messages

1- Man attains all his desires only in Paradise. «لهم فیها مایشاءون»

2- In Paradise, whatever they desire exists. «لهم فیها مایشاءون» But concerning the people of the Fire, we read: «و حیل بینهم و بین ما یشتهون» [253] (and a barrier will be placed between them and what they desire). A barrier will be placed between the people of the Fire and what they desire.

3- Allah has made the fulfillment of His promises obligatory upon Himself. «على ربّك وعداًمسئولا»

253) Saba', 54.

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ فَيَقُولُ ءَأَنتُمْ أَضْلَلْتُمْ عِبَادِى هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ أَمْ هُمْ ضَلُّوا۟ ٱلسَّبِيلَ (۱۷)
On the day that He will muster them and those whom they worship besides Allah, He will say, ‘Was it you who led astray these servants of Mine, or did they themselves stray from the way?’ (17)

Key Points

In the Quran, the factors leading to misguidance and deviation are introduced as follows: 1- Bad company. «لقد اَضلّنى عن الذّكر» [254], the bad friend led me astray. 2- Caprice and desire (Hawa). «و لا تَتّبع الهَوى فیضلّك عن سبیل اللّه» [255], do not follow your desires, for they will deviate you from the path of Allah. 3- Deviant scholars. «یَكتبون الكتاب بایدیهم ثمّ یَقولون هذا من عِنداللّه» [256], they write things with their own hands and then say this is from Allah. 4- Misguiding leaders. «وَ اضلّ فرعونُ قومَه» [257], Pharaoh misguided his people. 5- Satan. «انّه عدوٌّ مُضِلّ مُبین» [258], surely Satan is a clear misguider. 6- The misguided majority. «و اِن تُطِع اكثر مَن فى الارض یُضِلّوك عن سبیلِ اللّه» [259], if you obey the majority of those on earth, they will deviate you from the path of Allah. 7- Misguided parents. «انّا وَجَدنا آباءنا... و انّا على آثارهم مُقتدون» [260], indeed we found our fathers [worshipping idols]... and we are following in their footsteps.

The motivations for questioning are not uniform: A: Sometimes the question is for understanding. «فاسئلوا اهل الذّكر» [261] B: Sometimes the question is to rebuke others. «ءَانت قلت للنّاس اتّخذونى واُمّى الهَین من دون اللّه» [262] Allah rebukes Jesus (عليه السلام) to reprimand his followers, asking: Did you tell the people to take me and my mother as two gods besides "Allah"? C: Sometimes the question is to rebuke the person Himself. «ام هم ضلّوا السبیل»

254) Furqan, 29. 255) Sad, 26. 256) Baqarah, 79. 257) Taha, 79. 258) Qasas, 25. 259) An'am, 116. 260) Zukhruf, 23. 261) Nahl, 43 and Anbiya, 7. 262) Ma'idah, 116.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the polytheists and their objects of worship will be questioned. «یحشرهم و ما یعبدون فیقول...»

2- On the Day of Judgment, beings other than humans will also be resurrected, such as idols. «و ما یعبدون»

3- On the Day of Judgment, everything will gain consciousness and be addressed. «ءَانتم»

4- The worship of Allah exists in the innate nature (Fitrah) of all humans, and human deviation is accidental. «ءَانتم اضللتم... أم هم ضلّوا» (The word «السبیل» [the path] is also an indication that the known path is the natural and innate path.)

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ سُبْحَـٰنَكَ مَا كَانَ يَنۢبَغِى لَنَآ أَن نَّتَّخِذَ مِن دُونِكَ مِنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ وَلَـٰكِن مَّتَّعْتَهُمْ وَءَابَآءَهُمْ حَتَّىٰ نَسُوا۟ ٱلذِّكْرَ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًۢا بُورًا (۱۸)
They will say, ‘Immaculate are You! It does not behoove us to take any wali in Your stead! But You provided for them and their fathers until they forgot the Reminder, and they were a ruined lot.’ (18)

Key Points

The word «بُور» means destruction and corruption/ruin. A land that is devoid of trees, flowers, and plants is called «بایر» (barren). In this Ayah, it might be the plural of «بائر», or it might be a verbal noun (maṣdar), meaning a people who are entirely ruined/corrupt.

The word «سبحانك» is used in two instances: one when expressing astonishment, and the other when encountering deviant beliefs and statements.

The factors causing forgetfulness (nisyān) and heedlessness (ghaflah) are numerous in the Quran, and some of them include: A: Wealth and riches, which are mentioned in this Ayah. B: Children and family. «لا تُلهِكُم اموالكم و لا اولادكم عن ذكر اللّه» (263) C: Trade/Commerce. «لا تُلهِیهم تجارة و لا بَیع عن ذكراللّه» (264) D: Satan, discord, gambling, and wine. «انّما یُرید الشّیطان أن یُوقع بینكم العَداوة و البَغضاء فى الخمر و المَیسر و یَصُدّكم عن ذكر اللّه» (265)

(263) Al-Munafiqun, 9. (264) An-Nur, 37. (265) Al-Ma'idah, 91.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, the worshipped deities will speak. «قالوا»

2- Wherever polytheism (shirk) is mentioned, the glorification and purification of Allah is necessary. «سبحانك»

3- Allah does not withhold His blessings even from the deviants. «لكن متّعتهم»

4- The danger of wealth lies where it causes the forgetfulness of Allah, not that wealth is absolutely bad or dangerous. «متّعتهم... حتّى نسوا»

5- In families where several generations have been affluent, the ground for forgetting Allah and the Day of Judgment is greater. «و آبائهم»

6- Do not look at the wealth and displays of the affluent; all of them are destined for ruin. «كانوا قوماً بورا»

Ayah

فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوكُم بِمَا تَقُولُونَ فَمَا تَسْتَطِيعُونَ صَرْفًا وَلَا نَصْرًا ۚ وَمَن يَظْلِم مِّنكُمْ نُذِقْهُ عَذَابًا كَبِيرًا (۱۹)
So they will certainly impugn you in what you say, and you will neither be able to circumvent [punishment] nor find help, and whoever of you does wrong, We shall make him taste a terrible punishment. (19)

Key Points

Allah has mentioned effects (or consequences) for oppression (zulm) in the Quran, including: 1- Failure/Frustration. «واللّه لایُفلح الظالمون» [266] Allah does not allow the conspiracies of the oppressors to reach fruition. 2- Deprivation of Divine favors/grace. «الا لَعنة اللّه على الظالمین» [267] 3- Punishment/Torment. «الا اِنّ الظالمون فى عذاب مُقیم» [268] And also the Ayah under discussion; «نُذِقه عذاباً كبیرا» 4- Refusal to accept a substitute (ransom) for the punishment. «و لو انّ للّذین ظلموا ما فى الارض جَمیعا ومثله معه لافتَدَوا به» [269] 5 - Refusal to accept excuses. «لا یَنفع الظالمین مَعذرتهم» [270] But worse than the uselessness of the excuse is the refusal to grant permission to apologize/make excuses. «و لایُؤذن لهم فَیَعتذِرون» [271]

266) Tawbah, 19. 267) Hud, 18. 268) Shura, 45. 269) Zumar, 47. 270) Rum, 57. 271) Mursalat, 36.

Messages

1- Imaginary deities and idols are not only unable to help you, but they will also condemn and deny your actions. «فقد كذّبوكم»

2- The polytheist (mushrik) will have no support on the Day of Judgment and will be incapable of any action to save himself. «فما تستطیعون صَرفاً و لانَصرا»

3- The polytheist is an oppressor (zalim). (The phrase «وَ مَن یظلم» is situated within the context of «ومن یشرك».)

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَا قَبْلَكَ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ إِلَّآ إِنَّهُمْ لَيَأْكُلُونَ ٱلطَّعَامَ وَيَمْشُونَ فِى ٱلْأَسْوَاقِ ۗ وَجَعَلْنَا بَعْضَكُمْ لِبَعْضٍ فِتْنَةً أَتَصْبِرُونَ ۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ بَصِيرًا (۲٠)
We did not send any apostles before you but that they indeed ate food and walked in marketplaces. We have made you a [means of] test for one another, [to see] if you will be patient and steadfast, and your Lord is all-seeing. (20)

Key Points

In Ayah 7 of this Surah, the excuses made by the disbelievers regarding the ordinary life of the Prophet of Islam were mentioned. Allah says in this Ayah: all the Prophets ate food, lived with the people and among them, and walked in the marketplaces.

Messages

1- Sending the Prophets is among the divine programs and traditions (Sunan). «وماارسلنا قبلك من المرسلین»

2- Familiarity with history both disarms those who make excuses and provides solace to the Noble Prophet. «و ماارسلنا قبلك... انهم لیأكلون الطعام»

3- All the Prophets lived a life among the people. «لیأكلون الطعام و یمشون ...»

4- The educator (or mentor) must interact and socialize with the people. «یمشون فى الاسواق»

5- The simple life of the Prophets is a test for the people. «یمشون فى الاسواق - و جعلنا بعضكم لبعض فتنة» (Indeed, if the Prophets had lived a luxurious life with palaces and gold, people would have gathered around them out of worldliness, and such an inclination would have no value. The value lies in a person obeying someone who appears outwardly ordinary.)

6- One of the means of testing people is the people themselves. «جعلنا بعضكم لبعض فتنة» Sometimes a person is wealthy so that it may become known whether he is grateful or rebellious. And sometimes a person is poor so that he may be tested whether he is patient or envies the wealthy.

7- The key to success in divine tests is patience. «فتنة أتصبرون»

8- The philosophy behind divine tests is the nurturing of human beings. «ربّك»

9- In divine tests, nothing is discovered by Allah, because He is aware of everything. «و كان ربّك بصیرا» (His tests are for the flourishing of the inner qualities of man, because reward and punishment are based on the manifestation and emergence of those qualities.)

10- The knowledge that we are under the observation of Allah is the factor that generates patience. «أتَصبرون و كان ربّك بصیرا»

Ayah

۞ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْنَا ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ أَوْ نَرَىٰ رَبَّنَا ۗ لَقَدِ ٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ فِىٓ أَنفُسِهِمْ وَعَتَوْ عُتُوًّا كَبِيرًا (۲۱)
Those who do not expect to encounter Us say, ‘Why have angels not been sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?’ Certainly, they are full of arrogance within their souls and have become terribly defiant. (21)

Key Points

The word 'utūw (عُتوّ) means the worst type of oppression. [272] The Day of Resurrection is called the Day of Liqā’ (Encounter/Meeting) because on that day, negligence, ignorance, and other barriers are removed, and all people witness the Greatness of Allah. "و یعلمون ان اللّه هو الحقّ المبین" [273]

272) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 273) An-Noor, 25.

Messages

1- Disbelief in the Resurrection is the source of excuses made against the prophets. "لایرجون لقائنا لولا..."

2- Even hope in the Resurrection is sufficient for man's self-building and obedience to Allah. "لایرجون..."

3- The Day of Resurrection is the day of encountering Divine favors or Divine wrath (depending on which one people have prepared for themselves). "لقائنا"

4- The disbelievers believed in the angels. "لولا انزل علینا الملائكة"

5- Materialists consider everything to be material and seek to see Allah with the physical eye. "نرى ربّنا"

6- The source of man's rebellion and arrogance lies in his inner ambitions. "استكبروا فى انفسهم" (What is in the jar is what pours out of it.)

7- Sin and transgression have degrees. "عُتوّاً كبیراً"

Ayah

يَوْمَ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةَ لَا بُشْرَىٰ يَوْمَئِذٍ لِّلْمُجْرِمِينَ وَيَقُولُونَ حِجْرًا مَّحْجُورًا (۲۲)
The day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the guilty on that day, and they will say, ‘Keep off [from paradise]!’ (22)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about the arrogant ones (mustakbirīn) who had no hope in the Resurrection and expected the descent of the angels. In this Ayah, He says: the angels will descend upon them, but instead of carrying light and revelation, they will carry the severest threats. That day, according to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, is the day of their death, when Allah commands the Angel of Death (Malak al-Mawt) to seize the souls of the disbelievers, and he takes their lives. Just as the soul reaches their throats, the angels strike their faces and backs. Then, the Imam recited this Ayah. [274]

The word 'hijr' means prevention or restraint. Since intellect ('aql') restrains man from transgression, it is called 'hijr': «هل فى ذلك قَسمٌ لِذى حِجر» [275]. And a place that is walled off (stoned) and people are prevented from entering or exiting is called 'mahjūr'. Therefore, «حجراً مَحجوراً» means "a complete and absolute prohibition" (man'an man'an).

Seeing Allah is impossible, but seeing an angel is possible under certain conditions. «یوم یَرون الملائكة لا بُشرى یومئذ للمجرمین» Just as we read elsewhere: on the Day of Resurrection, the angels greet the believers, [276] and the inhabitants of Hell seek help from the angel appointed over Hell. [277]

274) Bihar, Vol. 8, p. 317. 275) Fajr, 5. 276) Nahl, 32. 277) Ghafir, 49.

Messages

1- Angels are seen under specific conditions. «یوم یرون»

2- The arrogant ones who expected the angels to descend upon them will receive no glad tidings from any quarter. «لا بشرى یومئذ للمجرمین»

Ayah

وَقَدِمْنَآ إِلَىٰ مَا عَمِلُوا۟ مِنْ عَمَلٍ فَجَعَلْنَـٰهُ هَبَآءً مَّنثُورًا (۲۳)
Then We shall attend to the works they have done and then turn them into scattered dust. (23)

Key Points

  • Haba' (هباء) refers to fine dust, and manthūr (منثور) means scattered. The Holy Quran, in another place, likens the deeds of the disbelievers to ashes that a strong wind scatters. «مَثلُ الّذین كفروا بربّهم اعمالهم كرِمادٍ اشتدّت به الرّیحُ فى یوم عاصِف لا یَقدرون ممّا كَسَبوا على شیى ء» [278] And in another Ayah, the work of the disbelievers is likened to a mirage, which a thirsty person mistakes for water. [279] In narrations, the examples of those whose deeds become void are identified as individuals who perform prayer and fasting but do not abstain from unlawful earnings or harbor malice and hatred toward Ali ibn Abi Talib علیهما السلام and his followers (Shi'a). [280]

278) Ibrahim, 18. 279) Nur, 39. 280) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqa'iq and Safi.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, human deeds will be embodied (take physical form). «ما عملوا من عمل فجعلناه هباء ...»

2- Nullification affects all the deeds of the criminals. «ما عملوا من عمل... هباء»

3- The destruction of deeds before a person's eyes is one of his regrets on the Day of Judgment. (Haba') Therefore, we should not be confident in our works and efforts, lest we meet a bad end and our deeds become ineffective.

4- The spirit of the deed is the motivation and intention of the one who performs it; otherwise, action devoid of belief and sincerity is fruitless and void. «هباء منثورا»

Ayah

أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَيْرٌ مُّسْتَقَرًّا وَأَحْسَنُ مَقِيلًا (۲٤)
On that day the inhabitants of paradise will be in the best abode and an excellent resting place. (24)

Key Points

| Sometimes the word «خَیر» (Khayr) and «أحسن» (Ahsan) does not mean "better," because its opposite is not good at all for this to be "better," similar to «قل ما عنداللّه خیر من اللهو» [281]. In this Ayah too, the word «خیر» and «احسن» means "good" and "excellent," because what was mentioned in the preceding Ayah was not good for this to be "better." The word «مَقیل» (Maqīl) means rest during midday, whether it involves sleep or not. [282]

281) Jumu‘ah, 11. 282) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Encouragement and glad tidings are necessary alongside warning and admonition. «هباء منثورا - خیر مستقر و احسن مقیلا»

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ تَشَقَّقُ ٱلسَّمَآءُ بِٱلْغَمَـٰمِ وَنُزِّلَ ٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ تَنزِيلًا (۲٥)
The day when the sky with its clouds will be split open, and the angels will be sent down [in a majestic] descent, (25)
ٱلْمُلْكُ يَوْمَئِذٍ ٱلْحَقُّ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ۚ وَكَانَ يَوْمًا عَلَى ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ عَسِيرًا (۲٦)
on that day true sovereignty will belong to the All-beneficent, and it will be a hard day for the faithless. (26)

Key Points

Since intercessions, apologies, wealth and children, family and status—none of these are effective for the salvation of the disbelievers, that Day will therefore be heavy upon them. The word «بالغَمام» might mean accompanied by clouds, meaning the sky will split open accompanied by clouds. The word «نزّل» implies that the descent of the angels on the Day of Resurrection is unexpected and a special descent.

Messages

1- The heaven was initially smoke, «ثمّ استوى الى السماء و هى دخان» (283), and in the future, it will disintegrate. «تشقّق السماء» (The sky splits open).

2- The abode of the angels is the heavens. «نزّل الملائكة» (The angels are sent down).

3- The ownership and sovereignty of Allah are real and true, not merely conventional, temporary, or tyrannical. «الملك یومئذ الحق» (The Sovereignty on that Day is the Truth).

4- The Resurrection is the day when Divine Mercy is manifested. «الملك یومئذ الحق للرحمن...» (The Sovereignty on that Day is the Truth, belonging to the Most Merciful...).

283) Fussilat, 11.

Ayahs

وَيَوْمَ يَعَضُّ ٱلظَّالِمُ عَلَىٰ يَدَيْهِ يَقُولُ يَـٰلَيْتَنِى ٱتَّخَذْتُ مَعَ ٱلرَّسُولِ سَبِيلًا (۲۷)
It will be a day when the wrongdoer will bite his hands, saying, ‘I wish I had followed the Apostle’s way! (27)
يَـٰوَيْلَتَىٰ لَيْتَنِى لَمْ أَتَّخِذْ فُلَانًا خَلِيلًا (۲۸)
Woe to me! I wish I had not taken so and so as a friend! (28)

Key Points

|

Ayah

لَّقَدْ أَضَلَّنِى عَنِ ٱلذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ إِذْ جَآءَنِى ۗ وَكَانَ ٱلشَّيْطَـٰنُ لِلْإِنسَـٰنِ خَذُولًا (۲۹)
Certainly he led me astray from the Reminder after it had come to me, and Satan is a deserter of man.’ (29)

Key Points

“Khidhlan” (abandonment/betrayal) is when a person hopes for the support of someone, but that person abandons him. According to narrations, the divine leader is the manifestation of Dhikr (remembrance/admonition), and being misled from Dhikr, as stated in «لقد اضلّنى عن الذكر», means being misled from the true leader. (284)

Islam has many recommendations regarding friendship and choosing friends, encouraging association with certain individuals and prohibiting association with others, a topic that requires an independent discussion. Some sub-topics concerning "the friend and friendship" are as follows: ways to recognize a friend, boundaries of friendship, continuing friendship, severing friendship, motives for friendship, etiquette of socializing with friends, and the rights of a friend. There are many verses and narrations concerning each of these, and we will mention a few:

If you are in doubt about recognizing someone, look at their companions to see who they are. «فانظروا الى خُلَطائه» (285)

Solitude is better than a bad companion. (286)

The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was asked: Who is the best friend? He replied: The one whose sight reminds you of Allah, whose speech increases your knowledge, and whose actions revive the remembrance of the Day of Judgment in you. (287)

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: When your power vanishes, your true companions will be distinguished from your enemies. (288)

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: A good companion is the best relative. (289)

It is stated in a Hadith: Test your friend regarding anger, Dirham (money), Dinar (money), and travel. If he succeeds in these tests, he is a good friend. (290)

In the poetry of poets, much has also been said about the friend and friendship, and socializing and companionship with good friends is highly recommended:

Your companion must be better than you, so that he may increase your intellect and faith.

And companionship with bad friends is condemned. A bad companion is likened to a beautiful, striped snake that holds deadly venom within:

Flee from a bad friend as much as you can, for a bad friend is worse than a bad snake. A bad snake only strikes your life, but a bad friend strikes your life and your faith.

Or a bad friend is likened to a dark cloud that covers the sun despite its greatness:

Do not sit with the wicked, for bad company, even if you are pure, will corrupt you. A piece of cloud can make the sun, despite its greatness, disappear.

284) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq. 285) Bihār, Vol. 74, p. 197. 286) Bihār, Vol. 77, p. 173. 287) Tafsir Qurtubi. 288) Ghurar al-Hikam. 289) Ghurar al-Hikam. 290) Bihār, Vol. 74, p. 180.

Messages

1- Inner regrets influence human actions and behavior. «یعضّ الظالم»

2- Abandoning the path of the Prophets is oppression (oppression against oneself and oppression against the Prophets). «یعضّ الظالم»

3- The punishment of the Day of Judgment is extremely severe. «یعضّ الظالم على یَدَیه» (The polytheist does not merely bite one finger or one hand, but bites both his hands.)

4- On the Day of Judgment, consciences awaken. «یعضّ - یقول»

5- Regret is proof of human free will; if a person were compelled, regret would be meaningless. «یالیتنى»

6- Illicit friendships today lead to a dangerous tomorrow. «یالیتنى» A friend influences human destiny.

7- Faith alone is not sufficient; accompanying the Prophets is also necessary. «مع الرسول»

8- Connection with the Prophets, even if slight, can be a path to salvation. «سبیلاً»

9- Two friendships cannot reside in one heart (friendship with the Prophet and friendship with a misguided person). «اتّخذت مع الرسول... لم اتّخذ فلاناً خلیلاً»

10- Deviant friends are agents of Satan. «فلاناً خلیلاً - و كان الشیطان»

11- The name and person of individuals are not important; their effectiveness is what matters. «فلاناً - اضلّنى»

12- The sign of a bad friend is that he makes a person heedless. «اضلّنى عن الذكر»

13- Sometimes the emotions of friendship overcome reasoning and logic. «اضلّنى عن الذكر بعد اذ جاءنى»

14- Allah completes the argument (establishes proof) against all people. «بعد اذ جاءنى»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلرَّسُولُ يَـٰرَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِى ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانَ مَهْجُورًا (۳٠)
And the Apostle will say, ‘O my Lord! Indeed my people consigned this Quran to oblivion.’ (30)

Key Points

This Ayah speaks of the complaint of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, and because He is the "Mercy to the worlds" (رحمة للعالمین), He does not curse. Imam Reza عليه السلام said: The reason we recite the Quran in prayer is so that the Quran is brought out of abandonment (mahjūriyyat). [291] It is narrated in traditions (Riwāyāt): Recite fifty verses of the Quran every day, and do not let your goal be reaching the end of the Surah; recite slowly and move your heart with the recitation of the Quran, and whenever tribulations attack you like a dark night, seek refuge in the Quran. [292] I felt it appropriate to quote the admissions of some great scholars regarding the abandonment of the Quran:

A: Mullā Ṣadrā قدس سره says in the introduction to his commentary on Sūrat al-Wāqi‘ah: I studied the books of philosophers extensively until I thought I was someone, but as soon as my insight opened slightly, I found myself empty of true knowledge. In the end of my life, I decided to pursue contemplation (tadabbur) in the Quran and the traditions of Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad علیهم السلام. I became certain that my work had been baseless, for throughout my life I had stood in the shadow instead of the light. My soul was set ablaze by grief and my heart caught fire, until Divine mercy took my hand and acquainted me with the secrets of the Quran, and I began interpreting and contemplating the Quran. I knocked on the door of revelation, the doors opened, the veils were lifted, and I saw the angels saying to me: «سلام علیكم طبتم فادخلوها خالدین». [293]

B: Fayḍ Kāshānī قدس سره says: I wrote books and treatises, and I conducted research, but in none of the sciences did I find a cure for my pain or water for my thirst. I feared for myself and fled and repented to God until Allah guided me through deep reflection upon the Quran and Hadith. [294]

C: Imam Khomeini قدس سره, in a statement, expressed regret that he had not spent his entire life on the path of the Quran, and he advised seminaries and universities to make the Quran and its various dimensions the highest goal in all fields, lest they regret their youth in their final days. [295]

Hijr (هِجر) includes separation by action, body, tongue, and heart. [296] The relationship between man and the Divine Book must be continuous and comprehensive in all areas, because the word hijr is used where there is a relationship between man and that thing. [297] Therefore, we must make an all-out effort to bring the Quran out of abandonment and make it our scientific and practical axis in all dimensions of life, so that we may gain the satisfaction of the beloved Prophet of Islam. Not reciting the Quran, preferring something other than the Quran over it, not making it the central focus, not contemplating (tadabbur) it, not teaching it to others, and not acting upon it are all instances of abandoning the Quran. Even someone who learns the Quran but sets it aside, does not look at it, and has no commitment to it, has also abandoned the Quran. [298]

291) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 292) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn. 293) Introduction to Tafsir Sūrat al-Wāqi‘ah. 294) Risālat al-Inṣāf. 295) Ṣaḥīfah Nūr, Vol. 20, p. 20 296) Mufradāt Rāghib. 297) Al-Taḥqīq fī Kalimāt al-Qur’ān. 298) Tafsir al-Munīr.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, one of the complainants will be the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله. «و قال الرسول»

2- This is a criticism of those who knowingly set the Quran aside. «اتّخذوا...» (They took...) and He did not say: «كان عندهم مهجورا» (It was abandoned by them).

3- The collection and compilation of the Quran took place during the time of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله Himself, and thus it is referred to and addressed. «هذَا القرآن» (This Quran)

4- The abandonment of the Quran, the Prophet's complaint, and our responsibility are certain. (Qāla [قال] is a past tense verb, indicating certainty.)

5- Mere superficial recitation is not enough; rather, the removal of abandonment is necessary. «مهجورا»

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ جَعَلْنَا لِكُلِّ نَبِىٍّ عَدُوًّا مِّنَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ ۗ وَكَفَىٰ بِرَبِّكَ هَادِيًا وَنَصِيرًا (۳۱)
That is how for every prophet We assigned an enemy from among the guilty, and your Lord suffices as helper and guide. (31)

Messages

1- The conflict between truth and falsehood operates within the orbit of Divine Will. «كذلك جعلنا»

2- The call for truth-seeking and truth-telling has always had opponents. «لكلّ نبىّ عدوّا»

3- Console yourselves by considering the difficulties faced by others. «لكل نبى»

4- Man is first contaminated by sin and gradually becomes an enemy of the prophets. «عدوّاًمن المجرمین»

5- Through sin, man separates himself from the path of truth. «من المجرمین» (The word jirm (جُرم) means separation.)

6- Hardships and opposition educate man. «جعلنا - ربّك»

7- The only way to overcome the enemy is adherence to Divine guidance and assistance. «كفى بربّك هادیاً و نصیراً»

8- In the face of opponents, we must rely upon God. «كفى بربّك هادیاً و نصیراً»

9- The Lord is sufficient for support and guidance, so why attach oneself to others? «كفى بربّك هادیاً»

10- Even the prophets are in need of Divine guidance. «كفى بربّك هادیاً»

11- In the conflicts between truth and falsehood, man needs two things: one is guidance, and the other is assistance. «هادیاً و نصیراً»

12- Guidance and assistance are among the attributes of Allah's Lordship. «ربّك هادیاً و نصیرا»

Ayah

وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَوْلَا نُزِّلَ عَلَيْهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ جُمْلَةً وَٰحِدَةً ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ لِنُثَبِّتَ بِهِۦ فُؤَادَكَ ۖ وَرَتَّلْنَـٰهُ تَرْتِيلًا (۳۲)
The faithless say, ‘Why has not the Quran been sent down to him all at once?’ So it was, that We may strengthen your heart with it, and We have recited it [to you] in a measured tone. (32)

Key Points

The fault-finders constantly raised new excuses. Sometimes they would say: Why is revelation not sent down to us? Sometimes they would say: Why do we not see the Angel of Revelation? Why does the Prophet not possess wealth, palaces, or gold? And sometimes they would say: Why is the Quran not revealed all at once?

While the Quran has two types of revelation: one is the instantaneous revelation (نزول دفعى) which was sent down upon the heart of the Prophet on the Night of Qadr (Laylat al-Qadr), and the other is the gradual revelation (نزول تدریجى) which was sent down over twenty-three years according to various occasions.

Effects and reasons for the gradual revelation of the Quran: 1- Gradual revelation is a factor for the continuous and constant connection of the Noble Prophet with the source of revelation, and a means of strengthening him on his path, indicating that the prophethood is not a momentary spark. 2- A living and comprehensive religion is one that has a strong connection with events and occasions, and events and occasions arise gradually, not all at once. 3- Performing all commands and abandoning all prohibitions instantly would have been difficult for the general public and would have caused hardship (عُسر و حَرَج). 4- Since every Surah and Ayah of the Quran is a miracle in itself, its gradual revelation is equivalent to several successive miracles, and every miracle is a source of comfort for the Prophet's heart against the harassment of enemies. 5- There is a great difference between the revelation of every Ayah according to a need that arises, and the revelation of all Ayahs without considering the needs. 6- Some Ayahs are answers to people's questions, so the question must first be raised before an Ayah is revealed in response to it. 7- Some Ayahs and rulings were revealed for a specific period, thus the abrogating Ayah (nāsikh) must be revealed later.

What is Tartīl? The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Tartīl is that you recite the Quran with clear articulation (not like poetry, nor like prose), that you pause when encountering its subtleties, purify your soul and heart, and that your goal in recitation is not merely reaching the end of the Surah. [299]

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The meaning of Tartīl is observing the rules of pausing (waqf) and pronouncing the letters correctly. [300]

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Tartīl means reciting the Quran beautifully and calmly, pausing in it, seeking refuge in Allah when reciting Ayahs related to the Fire and punishment, and supplicating when encountering Ayahs about Paradise and asking the Merciful God for Paradise. [301]

299) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān. 300) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq wa Manhaj al-Sādiqīn. 301) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The leader must have a confident, firm, and tranquil heart. «لنثبّت به فؤادك»

2- The result of prophetic training is the tranquility of hearts, not merely teaching and acquiring information. «لنُثبّت به فؤادك»

3- Education and training must be gradual. «و رتّلناه ترتیلا»

Ayah

وَلَا يَأْتُونَكَ بِمَثَلٍ إِلَّا جِئْنَـٰكَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَأَحْسَنَ تَفْسِيرًا (۳۳)
They do not bring you any representation but that We bring you the truth [in reply to them] and the best exposition. (33)

Key Points

The Holy Quran, addressing the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله in this Ayah, states: The opponents' argument is either falsehood, in which case We bring forth the word of truth for you in response, or it is truth, in which case We relate to you a word that is better and more comprehensive than their truth.

Messages

1- The Quran is a comprehensive book and answers all objections. «و لایأتونك بمثل الاّ جئناك بالحقّ...»

2- The Prophets were engaged with opponents and dealt with them through argumentation. «لایأتونك بمثل الاّ جئناك بالحقّ...»

3- Allah defends the Prophets and presents the word of truth against the false statements of the disbelievers. «و لا یأتونك بمثل الاّ جئناك» (and He did not say: «جئتَ» [you brought]).

4- The arguments and evidence of the opponents lack solidity. «بمثل»

5- Truth is victorious over falsehood. «لا یأتونك ... الا جئناك بالحق و احسن تفسیراً»

6- The goal of the Prophets is the establishment of truth. «جئناك بالحق»

7- Speech must be logical and truthful. «جئناك بالحق»

8- The knowledge and speech of the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are from Allah. «جئناك...»

9- We should answer the speech and objections of others with better discourse. «و احسن تفسیرا»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِينَ يُحْشَرُونَ عَلَىٰ وُجُوهِهِمْ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا (۳٤)
Those who will be mustered [fallen] on their faces toward hell, they are the worse situated and further astray from the [right] way. (34)

Messages

1- Humiliating the prophets is the cause of one's own humiliation on the Day of Resurrection. «یحشرون على وجوههم»

2- The most misguided people are those who raise objections against the prophets. «اضلّ سبیلا»

Ayahs

وَلَقَدْ ءَاتَيْنَا مُوسَى ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَجَعَلْنَا مَعَهُۥٓ أَخَاهُ هَـٰرُونَ وَزِيرًا (۳٥)
Certainly We gave Moses the Book and We made Aaron, his brother, accompany him as a minister. (35)
فَقُلْنَا ٱذْهَبَآ إِلَى ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَدَمَّرْنَـٰهُمْ تَدْمِيرًا (۳٦)
Then We said, ‘Let the two of you go to the people who have denied Our signs.’ Then We destroyed them utterly. (36)

Key Points

The word "تَدمیر" (Tadmir) comes from "دمار" (Damār), meaning astonishing destruction/annihilation. [302] The methods of propagation (Da'wah) and education in the Quran are varied; in one place, He says: one must go to the misguided, "اِذهبا" (Go forth, both of you), and in another place, He says: they must be commanded to present themselves so that their arrogance is broken. Just as Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) commanded the Queen of Sheba: "و آتونى مسلمین" [303] (and come to me in submission), meaning, present yourselves before us in complete submission.

302) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Quran. 303) Naml, 31.

Messages

1- Until we become familiar with the Divine Book, we do not have the right to propagate (the message) to others. "آتینا موسى الكتاب... اذهبا" (We gave Moses the Book... Go forth, both of you)

2- The successor of the prophets must also be appointed by Allah. "جعلنا معه اخاه..." (We appointed for him his brother...)

3- In great responsibilities, there is a need for a minister and a helper. "جعلنا معه اخاه هارون وزیرا" (We appointed for him his brother Aaron as a minister)

4- In some periods, there were multiple prophets, but the leader was one. "هارون وزیرا" (Aaron as a minister)

5- In propagation, sometimes individuals must be dispatched to preach together. "اِذهبا" (Go forth, both of you)

6- Establishing the conclusive proof (Itmām al-Hujjah) upon the misguided is necessary. "اِذهبا الى القوم الّذین كذّبوا" (Go forth, both of you, to the people who denied)

7- The denial and obstinacy of the people do not prevent the grace of Allah. (He sends the Prophet for their guidance) "اذهبا الى القوم الّذین كذّبوا" (Go forth, both of you, to the people who denied)

8- Denying the Prophet is denying Allah. "كذّبوا بآیاتنا" (They denied Our signs)

9- The result of denying the truth is utter destruction and eradication. "فدمّرناهم" (Then We utterly destroyed them)

10- Narrating the history and fate of previous disbelievers and describing their annihilation serves as a basis for warning and forbidding the evil (Nahy 'an al-Munkar) among the disbelievers of today. "فدمّرناهم" (Then We utterly destroyed them)

Ayah

وَقَوْمَ نُوحٍ لَّمَّا كَذَّبُوا۟ ٱلرُّسُلَ أَغْرَقْنَـٰهُمْ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ لِلنَّاسِ ءَايَةً ۖ وَأَعْتَدْنَا لِلظَّـٰلِمِينَ عَذَابًا أَلِيمًا (۳۷)
And Noah’s people, We drowned them when they impugned the apostles, and We made them a sign for mankind, and We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment. (37)

Messages

1- The denial of one prophet is equivalent to the denial of all prophets. «و قوم نوح لمّا كذّبوا الرّسل»

2- History is a lesson to be learned. «جعلناهم للناس آیة»

3- The destiny of human beings is in their own hands. «كذّبوا... اغرقناهم»

4- Denying the prophets is oppression. «و اعتدنا للظالمین»

5- Denying the prophets brings both worldly wrath, «اغرقنا», and otherworldly punishment. «عذاباً الیماً»

Ayahs

وَعَادًا وَثَمُودَا۟ وَأَصْحَـٰبَ ٱلرَّسِّ وَقُرُونًۢا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ كَثِيرًا (۳۸)
And ‘Ad and Thamud, and the people of Rass, and many generations between them. (38)
وَكُلًّا ضَرَبْنَا لَهُ ٱلْأَمْثَـٰلَ ۖ وَكُلًّا تَبَّرْنَا تَتْبِيرًا (۳۹)
For each of them We drew examples, and each We destroyed utterly. (39)

Key Points

"Qurūn" (قُرون) is the plural of "Qarn" (قَرن), which originally means a group of people living together at the same time. A period of forty to one hundred years is also called a Qarn (century). "Tatbīr" (تتبیر) means the loss of greatness and being overthrown, leading to destruction and annihilation. [304]

Who were the Companions of the Rass (Aṣḥāb al-Rass)? Much has been written in the commentaries regarding the people of "Rass," including: they were the same people as the people of Shu'ayb, or that "Rass" is a city in "Yamāmah," or a well in "Antioch," or the "Aras River," or something else. However, in the book Uyūn Akhbār al-Riḍā, we read that Imam Riḍā عليه السلام said: A person asked Hadrat Ali عليه السلام about the "Companions of the Rass." The Imam عليه السلام replied: After Noah's flood, a tree called the "King Tree" (Shāh-Darākht) was planted. The people (considered this tree sacred and) built twelve villages around it. The villages were named after the Iranian months: Farvardīn, Ordībehesht, Khordād, up to the twelfth month. They held festivals and celebrations in one of the villages every month, though their celebration in the village of Esfand was more magnificent, and a larger crowd gathered. During those festivities, they would set a tree on fire, and as soon as the smoke rose, they would weep and lament. Allah sent a prophet to them and guided them, but they denied him. Their prophet cursed them, and their sacred tree dried up. After that, they dug a deep well and threw the Prophet of God into it, thus martyring him. [305] And in Nahj al-Balāghah, He (Ali عليه السلام) says: «أینَ اصحابُ مدائن الرّس الّذین قَتلوا الانبیاء» (Where are the Companions of the cities of Rass who killed the Prophets?)

304) التحقیق فى كلمات القرآن. 305) تفسیر كنزالدقائق.

Messages

1- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) has been to send Messengers for the guidance of the people and the destruction of those who denied them. «كذّبوا الرسل اغرقناهم... و عاداً و ثمودَ...»

2- Allah has completed the argument (Ittmām al-Ḥujjah) with all nations. «و كلاً ضربنا له الامثال»

3- The wrath and anger of Allah upon the disbelievers is one of the Divine traditions. «و كلاً تبّرنا تتبیرا»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَتَوْا۟ عَلَى ٱلْقَرْيَةِ ٱلَّتِىٓ أُمْطِرَتْ مَطَرَ ٱلسَّوْءِ ۚ أَفَلَمْ يَكُونُوا۟ يَرَوْنَهَا ۚ بَلْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَرْجُونَ نُشُورًا (٤٠)
Certainly they must have passed the town on which an evil shower was rained. Have they not seen it? Rather, they did not expect resurrection [to happen]. (40)

Key Points

The intent of the village (qaryah) upon which the rain of calamity fell is the village of the people of Lot (قوم لوط), upon which a rain of stones fell. According to the statement of Imam Baqir عليه السلام, the name of their region was "Sodom." [306]

306) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Ancient ruins can be a means of admonition. «أفلم یكونوا یرونها» (Recounting history and narrating the fate of the misguided provides a context for propagation (Da'wah), education, and forbidding evil.)

He who learns nothing from the passage of time, will learn nothing from any teacher.

2- Arrogance against the truth and persistence in disbelief prevent the reception of the truth. «افلم یكونوا یرونها بل كانوا لا یرجون»

3- Only those who hold the light of hope in their hearts and believe in the Resurrection benefit from events and warnings. «بل كانوا لا یرجون نشورا»

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَأَوْكَ إِن يَتَّخِذُونَكَ إِلَّا هُزُوًا أَهَـٰذَا ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ ٱللَّهُ رَسُولًا (٤۱)
When they see you they just take you in derision: ‘Is this the one whom Allah has sent as an apostle!? (41)

Messages

1- Whoever possesses obstinacy and arrogance does not accept the truth. «الا هزواً»

2- Mockery and contempt for the prophets is the constant practice of the disbelievers. «و اذا رأوك... أهذا الّذى...»

3- By denying the person of the Prophet, the disbelievers undermined the message (prophethood). «أهذا الّذى بعث...»

Ayah

إِن كَادَ لَيُضِلُّنَا عَنْ ءَالِهَتِنَا لَوْلَآ أَن صَبَرْنَا عَلَيْهَا ۚ وَسَوْفَ يَعْلَمُونَ حِينَ يَرَوْنَ ٱلْعَذَابَ مَنْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا (٤۲)
Indeed he was about to lead us astray from our gods, had we not stood firm by them.’ Soon they will know, when they sight the punishment, who is further astray from the [right] way. (42)

Messages

1- The polytheists admit the influence of the words of the Prophet of Islam. «لیضلّنا عن آلهتنا»

2- Sometimes man thinks so perversely that he mistakes guidance for misguidance. «لیضلّنا عن آلهتنا»

3- Those who do not accept one conscious God will accept several unconscious idols as gods. «آلهتنا»

4- Patience and steadfastness are not valuable everywhere; sometimes they are obstinacy and stubbornness, such as patience and steadfastness in misguidance and deviation. «صَبَرنا»

5- The teachings of Islam attract non-stubborn people, and only the stubborn remain deprived. «لولا أن صَبَرنا علیها»

Ayah

أَرَءَيْتَ مَنِ ٱتَّخَذَ إِلَـٰهَهُۥ هَوَىٰهُ أَفَأَنتَ تَكُونُ عَلَيْهِ وَكِيلًا (٤۳)
Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god? Is it your duty to watch over him? (43)

Key Points

Any object of worship other than the Truth (Allah) is called hawa (desire/whim). [307] Following desires (hawa-parasti) from the perspective of the Quran and narrations: Following desires is the source of heedlessness. «و لاتُطِع مَن أغفَلنا قَلبه عن ذِكرنا واتّبع هَواه» [308] Following desires is the source of disbelief (kufr). «مَن لا یؤمن بها و اتّبع هَواه» [309] Following desires is the worst deviation. «و مَن أضلّ ممّن اتّبع هَواه» [310] Following desires prevents just judgment. «فَاحكُم بین النّاس بالحقّ و لا تتّبع الهَوى» [311] Following desires is the source of corruption. «لو اتّبَع الحقّ أهوائهم لفَسدتِ السّموات والارض» [312] Following desires is the source of sorrows. One who follows desires has no faith. One who follows desires is senseless (lacks intellect). The beginning of tribulations (fitan) is the following of whims and the creation of innovations (bid'at). Whims and desires make a person deaf and blind, taking away the ability to distinguish truth from falsehood. The bravest people are those who overcome their desires. The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: Hawa (desire) is named hawa because it casts its owner down. [313]

307) Tafsir Qurtubi. 308) Kahf, 28. 309) Taha, 16. 310) Qasas, 50. 311) Sad, 26. 312) Mu’minun, 71. 313) A collection of narrations from Mizan al-Hikmah.

Messages

1- The root of idol worship is the worship of desires. «صبرنا على الهتنا - اتّخذ الهه هواه»

2- The search for God exists in the innate nature (fitrah) of every human being, but he falls into error regarding the manifestation and the finding of the Truth. «اتّخذ الهه هواه»

3- The Prophets have no responsibility for the compulsory guidance of those who follow desires. «أفأنت تكون علیه وكیلا»

4- Man possesses free will. «أفأنت تكون علیه وكیلا»

5- Education and faith must be without compulsion or coercion. «أفأنت تكون علیه وكیلا»

Ayah

أَمْ تَحْسَبُ أَنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ يَسْمَعُونَ أَوْ يَعْقِلُونَ ۚ إِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا كَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ ۖ بَلْ هُمْ أَضَلُّ سَبِيلًا (٤٤)
Do you suppose that most of them listen or exercise their reason? They are just like cattle; indeed, they are further astray from the way. (44)

Key Points

When criticizing the disbelievers (kuffār), the Quran uses the word akthar (most/majority) to separate the account of those who will be guided from the rest.

Question: Why is the deviated human worse than livestock? Answer: 1- An animal cannot grow beyond what it is, but a human can, and the potential and means for growth are fully provided for him. 2- An animal does not possess intellect ('aql) to understand good and bad or to recognize truth and falsehood, but deviated humans, despite having intellect, disregard it and act based on whim. 3- An animal follows a fixed instinct, but the deviated human follows hundreds of whims and desires. 4- The deviated human both goes astray and justifies his deviation. 5- An animal is tame towards the one who shows it affection, but the deviated human commits disbelief (kufr) against the Merciful God and is extremely ungrateful. 6- Animals possess conscious glorification (tasbīh); «كلّ قد عَلِم صلاته و تَسبیحه» [314] But deviated humans are not people of remembrance (dhikr) and glorification (tasbīh) and are heedless of Allah. 7- Animals do not deserve divine punishment ('adhāb), but the deviated human will be afflicted by His wrath.

It came in a tradition that the Glorious Yazdān (God) created the world's creatures in three types: One group is entirely intellect, knowledge, and existence; it is the angel, and knows nothing but prostration. Another group is empty of knowledge, like the animal, fattening itself on grass. This third is the human being, half of him angel and half of him donkey. Those two groups are safe from conflict and ruin, but this human is in torment with two opponents. One group has become absolutely immersed, like Jesus, joined with the angel. The form of Adam, but the meaning Gabriel, freed from anger, desire, and idle talk. The other portion joined the donkeys, becoming pure anger and absolute lust. [315] The child of Adam is a wondrous mixture, kneaded from the angel and from the animal. If he goes toward the former, he becomes better than it; if he goes toward the latter, he becomes worse than it.

314) Noor, 41. 315) Mawlawi (Rumi).

Messages

1- For his guidance, man must heed the directives of the inner prophet (intellect/'aql) and the outer prophet. «ام تحسب اَنّ اكثرهم یسمعون او یعقلون»

2- Disbelief (kufr) and rejecting the call of the prophets is foolishness. «ام تحسب انّ اكثرهم یسمعون اویعقلون»

3- Man, who is the vicegerent (khalīfah) of Allah, becomes lower than an animal by ignoring his own nature (fitrah). «اِن هم الاّ كالانعام بل هم اضلّ سبیلا»

Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّكَ كَيْفَ مَدَّ ٱلظِّلَّ وَلَوْ شَآءَ لَجَعَلَهُۥ سَاكِنًا ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَا ٱلشَّمْسَ عَلَيْهِ دَلِيلًا (٤٥)
Have you not regarded how your Lord spreads the twilight? (Had He wished He would have made it stand still.) Then We made the sun a beacon for it. (45)
ثُمَّ قَبَضْنَـٰهُ إِلَيْنَا قَبْضًا يَسِيرًا (٤٦)
Then We retract it toward Ourselves, with a gentle retracting. (46)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The meaning of the extended shade (الظل) in the Ayah is the shade between the break of dawn (Fajr) and sunrise. [316] Although some have interpreted the shade to mean the shade of the night or the afternoon, considering the subsequent phrase where He says: «ثمّ جَعلنا الشّمس علیه دَلیلا» (Then We made the sun a guide over it), the statement of the Imam is acceptable.

The manner in which the shade is extended is the role of the Awliya (saints/Imams), For they are the proof of the light of God's Sun. [317] Do not travel this path without this proof; Say, "I do not love those that set," like Khalil (Abraham). [318]

316) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 317) Shahriar also says: By Ali, I swear by God, I came to know God. 318) Mawlawi (Rumi).

Messages

1- The best way for everyone to know Allah is through contemplation of the phenomena of existence, which have been created based on Divine wisdom. «ألم تر» (Have you not seen?)

2- The rotation of the Earth around itself and the sun is not accidental; it is wise and managed. «ألم تَر الى ربّك كیف مدّ الظل» (Have you not seen how your Lord extended the shade?)

3- Man is immersed in blessings, yet he does not look upon them with an eye of contemplation. «ألم تر الى ربّك كیف مدّ الظل»

4- The extension and prolonged duration of the shade is for the sake of nurturing, growth, and flourishing. «ربّك... مدّالظل» (If the light were direct or the shade permanent, everyone would either burn up or freeze.)

5- Although Allah has the power to create intense change and transformation in nature, He does not abandon His tradition (Sunnah). «ولوشاء» (And if He willed)

6- The continuation of the universe's rotation is in His hand. «ولوشاء لجعله ساكنا» (And if He willed, He could make it stationary.)

7- The recognition of shade is possible due to the existence of the sun and light, because everything is known through the existence of its opposite. «ثمّ جعلنا الشمس علیه دلیلا» (Then We made the sun a guide over it.)

8- The movement of existence and the functioning of nature are the actions of Allah. «مدّ - جعلنا - قبضنا» (extended - We made - We withdrew)

9- Just as the creation of the world took place gradually, slowly, and in six stages, its change will also be gradual. «قبضاً یسیراً» (a gradual withdrawal)

10- Withdrawing anything is not difficult for Allah. «قبضاً یسیراً»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ لِبَاسًا وَٱلنَّوْمَ سُبَاتًا وَجَعَلَ ٱلنَّهَارَ نُشُورًا (٤۷)
It is He who made for you the night as a covering and sleep for rest and He made the day a recall to life. (47)

Key Points

In the Noble Qur'an, several things are mentioned as "garments" (libās): the night, «جعل لكم اللیل لباسا» [319], the spouse; «هُنّ لباس لكم وانتم لباس لهنّ» [320], and piety (taqwā) «ولباس التّقوى ذلك خیر» [321]. Sabt (سَبت) means the cessation and suspension of work. Rest after effort is also called sabt. [322] Nushūr (نُشور), derived from nashr (نَشر), means the spreading out of people during the day for work and effort.

319) Al-Furqan, 47. 320) Al-Baqarah, 187. 321) Al-A'raf, 26. 322) Al-Tahqiq fi Kalimat al-Qur'an.

Messages

1- The rotation of night and day is not accidental; wise goals and plans are inherent within it. «جعل لكم اللیل لباسا والنوم سُباتا»

2- The phenomenon of night is a divine blessing for humanity. «لكم»

3- We should harmonize our sleep and wakefulness with nature. «اللیل لباسا والنوم سُباتا» Darkness and silence are effective in creating tranquility, just as the presence of light is effective in human effort and activity.

4- Night preserves the body and health, just as clothing preserves the body. «اللیل لباسا»

5- The rotation of night and day, the tranquility during the night, and the effort during the day are signs of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّك كیف مدّ الظل... و هوالّذى...»

Ayahs

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَرْسَلَ ٱلرِّيَـٰحَ بُشْرًۢا بَيْنَ يَدَىْ رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ وَأَنزَلْنَا مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ مَآءً طَهُورًا (٤۸)
And it is He who sends the winds as harbingers of His mercy, and We send down from the sky purifying water, (48)
لِّنُحْـِۧىَ بِهِۦ بَلْدَةً مَّيْتًا وَنُسْقِيَهُۥ مِمَّا خَلَقْنَآ أَنْعَـٰمًا وَأَنَاسِىَّ كَثِيرًا (٤۹)
with which We revive a dead country and provide water to many of the cattle and humans We have created. (49)

Key Points

Ṭahūr (طهور) refers to something that is both ultimately pure and a purifier. The word balad (بلد) means land or territory, whether it is a city, a village, or a farm, based on Ayah 58 of Surah Al-A'raf, which states: «و البَلَد الطیّبُ یَخرُج نَباته باِذنِ ربّه» (And the good land—its vegetation comes forth by permission of its Lord). It is clear that the growth of vegetation is not exclusive to cities. The word anāsiyy (اناسىّ) is the plural of insiyy (اِنسىّ), synonymous with the meaning of insān (human being). Since the meaning of balad (بَلَد) and baldah (بَلدة) is the same, in describing it, He said: «میتاً» (masculine form) and did not say: «میتة» (feminine form). (323) In the utilization of water, the largest share is allocated to agriculture, followed by animals which benefit the most, and then human beings. For this reason, the noble Ayah first mentions agriculture and the revival of the earth, then animals, and finally humans. Winds have many benefits, including: purifying the air, transporting clouds, reducing heat, and fertilizing plants.

323) Tafsir Qurtubi.

Messages

1- The movement of the winds is by the will of Allah. «هوالّذى ارسل الریاح»

2- Winds have different types and are sometimes accompanied by rain. «ارسل الریاح... انزلنا...»

3- The descent of rain from the clouds is by the will of Allah. «انزلنا»

4- Water is both pure and a purifier; it cleanses the human body and other objects, and in wuḍū' (ablution) and ghusl (ritual bath), it is a source of purity for the human soul. «طهوراً»

5- Human life is indebted to plants and animals. (The revival of the earth and the watering of livestock are mentioned before the watering of humans.)

6- The works of Allah are accomplished through natural means. «ارسل الریاح - انزلنا - لنحیى»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ صَرَّفْنَـٰهُ بَيْنَهُمْ لِيَذَّكَّرُوا۟ فَأَبَىٰٓ أَكْثَرُ ٱلنَّاسِ إِلَّا كُفُورًا (٥٠)
Certainly We distribute it among them so that they may take admonition. But most people are only intent on ingratitude. (50)

Key Points

It is possible that the meaning of taṣrīf (تصریف) is the movement and distribution of rain, meaning We sent the rain repeatedly and scattered, sometimes with fine drops and sometimes with large drops, sometimes fast and sometimes slow, and all these changes and variations are for remembrance (tadhakkur) and gratitude (shukr), but alas... And it is possible that the meaning of the phrase "صرّفنا بینهم" (We have distributed/varied it among them) is the repetition and explanation of the various forms of the verses of the Quran, a meaning that appears frequently in the Quran. "واللّه العالم" (And Allah is the Knower).

Messages

1- Remembrance (or reminding) must be repeated and varied. "صرّفنا"

2- The Quran is the Book of guidance, and repetition and remembrance are prerequisites for guidance. "صرّفنا... لیذّكّروا"

3- Remembering blessings is gratitude for those blessings, and heedlessness toward them is ingratitude (kufrān) toward them. "لیذّكّروا... كفورا"

4- Those who are grateful for blessings are few. "فَابى اكثر الناس الاّ كفورا"

Ayah

وَلَوْ شِئْنَا لَبَعَثْنَا فِى كُلِّ قَرْيَةٍ نَّذِيرًا (٥۱)
Had We wished, We would have sent a warner to every town. (51)

Key Points

This Ayah, in a way, expresses the finality of the Prophet of Islam and the comprehensiveness of His school (Maktab), because it states: No prophet has been sent anywhere alongside you, for in your school and personality lies the capacity to manage the entire society, and there is no need for another. You possess such a status that with your presence, the era of prophethood concludes.

Messages

1- Although a leader is necessary for every nation, «و اِنْ من اُمّة الاّ خَلا فیها نذیر» [324], if there were a prophet in every village, this action would harm the unity of the nation's leadership. «ولو شئنا لبعثنا...»

2- The mission (Risalah) is a matter determined by divine decree (Tawqifi), not contractual or elective. «ولو شئنا لبعثنا...»

324) Fatir, 24.

Ayah

فَلَا تُطِعِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ وَجَـٰهِدْهُم بِهِۦ جِهَادًا كَبِيرًا (٥۲)
So do not obey the faithless, but wage a great jihad against them with it. (52)

Key Points

Our interaction with the disbelievers (kuffār) has several stages: First: Non-obedience to them. «فلا تُطِع» (So do not obey). Second: Struggle and Jihad. «و جاهِدهم» (And strive against them). Jihad and struggle are also of several types: Jihad with the sword, which is the Minor Jihad (Jihad Asghar), and intellectual Jihad using the logic of the Quran and responding to the doubts (shubuhāt) of the enemies, which is the Major Jihad (Jihad Kabir).

Messages

1- Compromise with the disbelievers is forbidden. «فلا تطع» (Resolutely reject any deviant demand made by the disbelievers.)

2- You can only be independent, resistant, and firm against the disbelievers when you are unified (focused). «لوشئنا لبعثنا فى كل قریة نذیراً - فلا تطع» (Had We willed, We would have sent a warner to every town - So do not obey). This means that now that the leadership is concentrated in the person of one Prophet, you are strong, so do not compromise.

3- Not following the disbelievers is insufficient; Jihad is necessary, and it must be a great and comprehensive Jihad. «فلا تُطِع ... و جاهدهم به جهاداً كبیرا»

4- Passive resistance is one of the methods of struggle. (By abandoning obedience to the disbelievers, strive against them.) «فلاتُطِع... و جاهدهم»

5- The Quran is the best tool for intellectual and cultural Jihad and the most powerful means of debate and argumentation with the enemies of Islam. «جاهدهم به»

6- Your enemies are striving for cultural and intellectual invasion; you should not be idle either. «جاهدهم» (Strive against them) implies reciprocity.

7- Intellectual and logical Jihad against disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk) is the greatest Jihad. «جهاداً كبیراً»

Ayah

۞ وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى مَرَجَ ٱلْبَحْرَيْنِ هَـٰذَا عَذْبٌ فُرَاتٌ وَهَـٰذَا مِلْحٌ أُجَاجٌ وَجَعَلَ بَيْنَهُمَا بَرْزَخًا وَحِجْرًا مَّحْجُورًا (٥۳)
It is He who merged the two seas: this one sweet and agreeable, and that one briny and bitter, and between the two He set a barrier and a forbidding hindrance. (53)

Key Points

The word maraj means mixing and setting two seas flowing until they meet each other. [325] ‘Adhb means palatable (or sweet), and furāt refers to very palatable water tending toward sweetness. Milh means salty, and ujāj means very salty water tending toward bitterness.

325) As we read in Ayah 19 of Sūrat Ar-Raḥmān: «مَرَج البَحرَین یَلتقیان».

Messages

1- How can the God Who does not allow palatable water and salty water, which are side by side, to mix, allow truth and falsehood, and disbelief and faith, to become intertwined? «فلا تُطِع الكافرین و جاهدهم به جهاداً كبیرا... مَرَج البحرین...»

2- By the will of Allah, even if liquids are side by side, they do not mix with each other. «جعل بینهما برزخاً»

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ مِنَ ٱلْمَآءِ بَشَرًا فَجَعَلَهُۥ نَسَبًا وَصِهْرًا ۗ وَكَانَ رَبُّكَ قَدِيرًا (٥٤)
It is He who created the human being from water, then invested him with ties of blood and marriage, and your Lord is all-powerful. (54)

Key Points

| The meaning of 'water' (ماء) in this Ayah is human semen, based on the verse: «ألم نخلقكم من ماء مهین» (326) and the verse: «خُلِقَ من ماء دافِق» (327). Nasab (نَسَب) is the bond that comes into existence through procreation, such as the bond between a father and son, or between siblings. The word Sihr (صِهر) means son-in-law, and affinity (being a son-in-law) is the bond that comes into existence between a man and another family, such as the bond between a son-in-law and his wife's relatives, which is technically called kinship by affinity.

326) Al-Mursalat, 20. 327) At-Tariq, 6.

Messages

1- Man is from water, «خلق من الماء بشرا», and so is the animal, «واللّه خلق كلّ دابّة من ماء» (328), and so are all other living creatures. «و جعلنا من الماء كلّ شى ء حىّ» (329)

2- All human beings were created from one substance, «خلق من الماء بشرا», therefore, racial distinctions and superiorities are baseless.

3- Man is a magnificent creature. «بشراً» (The tanwin indicates magnificence).

4- Human life is structured based on bonds of lineage and affinity. «نَسباً و صِهراً»

5- In this Ayah, relatives by lineage are mentioned before relatives by affinity, which is a sign of their priority. «نَسباً و صِهراً»

328) An-Nur, 45.

329) Al-Anbiya, 30.

Ayah

وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُهُمْ وَلَا يَضُرُّهُمْ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱلْكَافِرُ عَلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ ظَهِيرًا (٥٥)
They worship besides Allah that which neither brings them any benefit nor causes them any harm, and the faithless one is ever an abettor against his Lord. (55)

Messages

1- An action is valuable if it is followed by attracting a benefit or repelling a harm. The worship of other than Allah has no value. «ویعبدون من دون اللّه ما لاینفعهم و لایضرّهم»

2- Polytheists have no reason or logic for worshipping idols. «لاینفعهم ولایضرّهم»

3- Benefit and harm are only in the hand of Allah, not others. «لا ینفعهم و لا یضرّهم»

4- Polytheists are disbelievers (Kafir) and support one another in disbelief. «كان الكافر على ربّه ظهیرا»

Ayah

وَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ إِلَّا مُبَشِّرًا وَنَذِيرًا (٥٦)
We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner. (56)

Key Points

| In this short Ayah, there is a reference to Monotheism (Tawhid), "أرسلنا" (We sent), to Prophethood, "ك" (you, referring to the Prophet), and to the Resurrection (Ma'ad), "مبشّراً و نذیرا" (as a bearer of good news and a warner). Since the previous Ayah spoke about the opposition of the disbelievers (kuffar) and polytheists (mushrikin) and their mutual support, perhaps this Ayah is meant to console the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, [telling him] not to worry, because his duty is nothing but to give good news and warning.

Messages

1- The Prophets are responsible for their duty, not for the result. «و ما ارسلناك الا مبشّراً و نذیرا»

2- In guidance (irshad) and propagation (tabligh), warning and good news are effective when presented together. «مبشّراً و نذیرا»

3- The Prophets do not have the right to compel people or dominate them. «الاّ مبشّراً و نذیرا»
