4- Man needs warning more than good news. (The word "نذیر" is the intensive form [sighat al-mubalaghah] and indicates emphasis.) «نذیرا»

Ayah

قُلْ مَآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِلَّا مَن شَآءَ أَن يَتَّخِذَ إِلَىٰ رَبِّهِۦ سَبِيلًا (٥۷)
Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it, except that anyone who wishes should take the way to his Lord.’ (57)

Key Points

The Quran uses several types of expressions regarding the reward for the efforts of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, all of which are brought together in the Du'a Nudbah (Supplication of Lamentation): My reward is upon Allah. «اِن اجرىَ الاّ على اللّه» (330) My reward is the love of My Ahl al-Bayt (Household). «الاّ المَودّة فى القُربى» (331) My reward is your taking the path toward Allah. «یتّخذ الى ربّه سبیلا» (332) The reward I asked for is for your own benefit. «و ما سئلتكم من اجر فهو لكم» (333) This is because the affection (mawaddah) for the infallible leaders is the key and code for taking the path toward Allah, and the benefit people derive from choosing such a path is for their own good. Therefore, there is no material gain involved for the Prophet himself.

330) Saba, 47. 331) Shura, 23. 332) Furqan, 57. 333) Saba, 47.

Messages

1- A leader is successful when He announces His lack of expectation (of reward) to the people. «قل ما اسئلكم»

2- A person achieves sincerity (Ikhlas) when he seeks no material, titular, or verbal reward whatsoever. «ما اسئلكم من اجر»

3- Man is free in choosing his path and belief. «من شاء أن یتّخذ»

4- The reward of the Prophets is that people worship Allah. «الى ربّه سبیلا»

Ayah

وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى ٱلْحَىِّ ٱلَّذِى لَا يَمُوتُ وَسَبِّحْ بِحَمْدِهِۦ ۚ وَكَفَىٰ بِهِۦ بِذُنُوبِ عِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرًا (٥۸)
Put your trust in the Living One who does not die, and celebrate His praise. He suffices as one all-aware of the sins of His servants. (58)

Key Points

The word "Tawakkul" (reliance/trust) and its derivatives have appeared seventy times in the Quran. Tawakkul means relying upon Allah and taking Him as the Trustee (Wakil) in all affairs. In the previous Ayah, He told the Prophet to say to the people: "I ask no reward from you." It is as if in this Ayah He says: Religious leaders who do not covet people's wealth must rely solely on Allah for their own lives. Reliance upon Allah does not mean abandoning effort and striving. As Mawlawi (Rumi) says:

The Prophet said in a loud voice: "With reliance, tie the camel's knee." Hear the secret: "The earner is the beloved of Allah." Do not become lazy in seeking means due to reliance. Go, rely (Tawakkul) while earning, O uncle; Strive and earn, detail by detail.

Messages

1- Reliance of the mortal upon the mortal is futile. «توكّل على الحىّ الّذى لایموت»

2- Reliance of the weak upon the weak is futile. «توكّل على... الّذى خلق السموات»

3- Reliance of the ignorant upon the ignorant is futile. «توكّل على الحىّ الّذى... خبیرا»

4- Even the Prophets need divine admonition and reminder. «و توكّل»

5- Eternal life belongs exclusively to Allah. «الحىّ الّذى لایموت»

6- Take as your Trustee a God Who is eternally alive, and if He grants the enemy respite today, perhaps so they might cease their obstruction and disbelief, He will have the opportunity and time tomorrow to take vengeance upon them. «لایموت»

7- Inner reliance (Tawakkul) must be accompanied by practical glorification (Tasbih) and praise (Hamd). «و سبّح بحمد»

8- Allah has precise knowledge of the detailed actions of people. «بذنوب عباده»

9- Sinners are also the servants of Allah. «ذنوب عباده»

10- A person's belief that Allah is aware of his sins is the best leverage for the emergence of piety (Taqwa). «بذنوب عباده»

11- Allah's awareness of people's sins, besides being a threat to the disbelievers, is also a consolation and comfort for the Prophet, meaning: "Do not worry, We are aware of their obstructions and We will settle the account of the stubborn disbelievers." «و كفى به بذنوب عباده خبیراً»

Ayah

ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۚ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ فَسْـَٔلْ بِهِۦ خَبِيرًا (٥۹)
He, who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, and then settled on the Throne, the All-beneficent; so ask someone who is well aware about Him. (59)

Key Points

Regarding the creation of the universe in six days, the Quran mentions it six times. The meaning of six days is either six periods and stages or a duration equal to six natural days. Molavi says:

With deliberation, it was separated from the world, Until in six days, these earth and spheres were formed. Otherwise, He was capable, through 'Be, and it is' (Kun Fayakun), To bring forth a hundred earths and spheres. This deliberation is for your glorification; Be patient in the work, which comes slowly but correctly.

The word ‘Arsh (Throne) in Islamic culture is the center of command and administration of creation. The meaning of «استوى على العرش» (He settled Himself upon the Throne) is that very divine dominance and administration, based on Ayah 3 of Surah Yunus, which states: «ثمّ استوى على العرش یُدبّر الاَمر» (Then He settled Himself upon the Throne, administering the matter).

Divine Mercy is expansive over all things: 1- The system of existence and the creation of the universe is based on Mercy. «خلق السموات والارض... الرحمن» 2- The system of human creation is based on Mercy. «الاّ مَن رحم ربّى و لذلك خلقهم» (334) 3- The system of legislation and law-making is based on Mercy. «الرّحمن علّم القرآن» (335) 4- The system of Resurrection and accountability is based on Mercy. «كتب على نفسه الرحمة لیجمعنّكم» (336)

334) Hud, 119. 335) Ar-Rahman, 1-2. 336) Al-An'am, 12.

Messages

1- The creation of existence occurred gradually. «خلق... فى ستّة ایّام»

2- Allah both created the universe and has complete dominance over it. «خلق... ثم استوى»

3- The administration of existence is based on Divine Mercy. «ثم استوى على العرش الرّحمن»

4- Ask for something from Him Who is the possessor of Mercy. «الرّحمن فاسئل به»

5- Asking questions is not a fault; ignorance is the fault. «فاسئل به خبیرا»

6- Referring to an expert in Divine knowledge is necessary. «فاسئل به خبیرا»

Ayah

وَإِذَا قِيلَ لَهُمُ ٱسْجُدُوا۟ لِلرَّحْمَـٰنِ قَالُوا۟ وَمَا ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنُ أَنَسْجُدُ لِمَا تَأْمُرُنَا وَزَادَهُمْ نُفُورًا ۩ (٦٠)
When they are told: ‘Prostrate yourselves before the All-beneficent,’ they say, ‘What is ‘‘the All-beneficent’’? Shall we prostrate ourselves before whatever you bid us?’ And it increases their aversion. (60)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the word "مَا" (mā) has been mentioned twice, which is typically used for inanimate objects. The goal of the disbelievers in using this expression was to belittle the Sacred Divine Essence. Therefore, instead of [asking] "و مَن الرّحمن" (And who is the Most Merciful?), they said: "ما الرّحمن" (What is the Most Merciful?). And instead of [asking] "لمَن تأمرنا" (For whom do you command us?), they said: "لما تأمرنا" (For what do you command us?). The word "الرحمن" (Ar-Rahman) is the symbol of eternal and endless mercy and is among the exclusive attributes of the Lord. (337)

337) Tafsir Atayab al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Prostration (Sujud) is the manifestation of monotheism and the spirit of the monotheistic religions. "اسجدوا" (Prostrate!) (Among all duties, only prostration has been mentioned.)

2- When enjoining good (Amr bil Ma'ruf), point to the reason for your command. "اسجدوا للرحمن" (Prostrate to the Most Merciful!) (The reason for your prostration is the vast mercy of Allah.)

3- Disbelief and obstinacy cause the invitation of the prophets not only to be ineffective but also to increase the aversion and hatred of the disbelievers toward the word of truth. "زادهم نفوراً" (It only increased their aversion.)

Ayah

تَبَارَكَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ بُرُوجًا وَجَعَلَ فِيهَا سِرَٰجًا وَقَمَرًا مُّنِيرًا (٦۱)
Blessed is He who appointed houses in the sky and set in it a lamp and a shining moon. (61)

Key Points

The word "تبارك" (Tabārak) is used only in the past tense form (māḍī) and appears three times in this Surah. (In verses 1, 10, and this very verse). As mentioned, it is either derived from "بَرَك" (barak), meaning fixed and enduring, or from "بركة" (barakah), meaning abundant goodness. According to Imam Baqir عليه السلام, the meaning of "بروج" (burūj) in this verse is the stars. [338] "سراج" (sirāj) means the sun, whose light originates from itself, as we read: "وجعلنا الشمس سراجا" [339] If its light originates from an external source, it is called "مُنیر" (munīr). [340]

338) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 339) Nuh, 16. 340) Tafsir Namūneh.

Messages

1- The creation of the stars, the sun, and the moon is a manifestation of Allah's blessings. "تبارك الّذى..." (Tabārak al-ladhī...)

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ ٱلَّيْلَ وَٱلنَّهَارَ خِلْفَةً لِّمَنْ أَرَادَ أَن يَذَّكَّرَ أَوْ أَرَادَ شُكُورًا (٦۲)
It is He who made the night and the day alternate for someone who desires to take admonition, or desires to give thanks. (62)

Key Points

The word khalifah (خلفة) means the replacement of one thing by another. [341] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whatever voluntary night prayers you missed, you may compensate for them during the day. Then He recited this Ayah: «و هوالّذى جعل ...» [342] Drawing lessons of Monotheism (Tawhid) from the system governing existence depends on our will. How many are those who spend their lives researching a phenomenon, but because they lack the will to take heed, they learn no lesson from it. Such individuals resemble a mirror seller who constantly looks into the mirror but does not straighten his clothes, while a passerby might straighten his collar with just one attentive glance. Therefore, merely looking is not enough; the will to reform is also necessary. With this statement, the answer to the question of why some natural scientists are not God-cognizant, despite having spent a lifetime exploring nature, becomes clear: because they did not undertake their research with the intention of knowing the Creator. They are like a carpenter who has built many ladders but has not willed himself to climb them.

341) Mufradat Raghib. 342) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Man's will and desire are the foundation for his growth. «لمن اراد ان یذّكر»

2- Man is a being endowed with free will. «لمن اراد»

3- Man possesses an innate nature (fitrah) that is awakened through remembrance (tadhakkur). «یذكر» (Tadhakkur is used when a person inherently knew something but has forgotten it.)

4- Both knowledge and action (and gratitude) require will. («أَرادَ» is repeated)

5- Night and day are blessings worthy of gratitude and thanks. «اراد شكورا»

6- The remembrance of Allah is valuable only when it is based on awareness and insight. «اراد أن یذكر»

Ayahs

وَعِبَادُ ٱلرَّحْمَـٰنِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَمْشُونَ عَلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ هَوْنًا وَإِذَا خَاطَبَهُمُ ٱلْجَـٰهِلُونَ قَالُوا۟ سَلَـٰمًا (٦۳)
The servants of the All-beneficent are those who walk humbly on the earth, and when the ignorant address them, say, ‘Peace!’ (63)
وَٱلَّذِينَ يَبِيتُونَ لِرَبِّهِمْ سُجَّدًا وَقِيَـٰمًا (٦٤)
Those who spend the night for their Lord, prostrating and standing [in worship]. (64)

Key Points

In these verses, twelve characteristics of the special attributes of ‘Ibad ar-Rahman (the Servants of the Most Merciful) are mentioned, some of which are related to belief, some to social conduct, and some to ethics. Perhaps the intent behind mashi (walking) and moving calmly upon the earth is not merely the manner of walking, but also includes a balanced way of life. Hawn means both humility, forbearance, and gentleness, as well as tranquility and dignity (sakinah wa waqar). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The intent of hawna is living based on the innate nature (fitrah) and in harmony with the spirit that Allah has created. That is, the servants of Allah do not subject themselves to formality, hardship, or distress. (343) The word salam (peace) here means the greeting of farewell to vain talk and a peaceful interaction. Just as we read concerning Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام: when his uncle rejected him and told him to go away from him, Ibrahim said: «سلام علیك سأستغفر لك» (344) Goodbye, I shall soon ask forgiveness for you from the presence of Allah. Baytūtah means staying awake at night (vigil), whether for the entire night, half of it, or a portion of it. Just as one of the rituals of Hajj is the baytūtah of the pilgrims and Hajjis on the eleventh night and the twelfth night of Dhu al-Hijjah in the land of Mina, and the jurists consider the duration of baytūtah in that place to be half the night. Imam Ali عليه السلام, describing the pious, says: «امّا اللیل فصافّون اقدامهم» They stand firm at night in prayer and vigil; they are the worshippers of the night and the lions of the day. (345)

343) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 344) Maryam, 46 to 47. 345) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon Hammam.

Messages

1- The highest medal for a human being is the medal of servitude to Allah. «و عبادالرحمن» Because affiliation with the Infinite elevates man.

2- Everyone's behavior indicates their personality. «عبادالرحمن ... یمشون على الارض هونا» (The special servants of Allah are manifestations of humility.)

3- Islam is a comprehensive religion that even has a program for how one should walk. «یمشون على الارض هونا»

4- A person's faith and belief influence his personal behavior. «عبادالرحمن... یمشون»

5- Humility is the fruit of servitude and its first sign. «هوناً»

6- The source of forbearance and gentleness in the servants of Allah is faith, not their fear or weakness. «عباد الرحمن الّذین یمشون...»

7- Dignity and gentleness are among the most prominent characteristics of a believer. «یمشون... هونا»

8- Humility is necessary towards all people (towards men and women, young and old, knowledgeable and ignorant). «یمشون... هونا... قالوا سلاما»

9- Do not retaliate against the ignorant. «اذا خاطبهم الجاهلون قالوا سلاما»

10- Forbearance, patience, and tolerance are prominent characteristics of believers. «قالوا سلاما»

11- Humility must be present in action, «یمشون... هونا», in speech, «قالوا سلاما», and in worship. «سجداً و قیاماً»

12- Do not argue with ignorant and base individuals. «قالوا سلاما» (They speak words befitting their status, but you should speak words that are wise and noble.)

13- The best time for worship is the night, and there is a strong connection between the night, prayer, and supplication. «یَبیتُون لربّهم» (A calm atmosphere, distance from hypocrisy, and concentration of thought are among the blessings of the night.)

14- Night vigil and the continuity and persistence of worship are signs of the special servants of Allah. (The present tense verb «یَبیتُون» indicates continuity.)

15- What gives value to worship is sincerity. «لربّهم»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا ٱصْرِفْ عَنَّا عَذَابَ جَهَنَّمَ ۖ إِنَّ عَذَابَهَا كَانَ غَرَامًا (٦٥)
Those who say, ‘Our Lord! Turn away from us the punishment of hell. Indeed its punishment is enduring. (65)
إِنَّهَا سَآءَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا (٦٦)
Indeed it is an evil station and abode.’ (66)

Key Points

The word "غَرام" (Gharām) originally means a calamity from which a person has no escape, and it places a type of commitment and obligation upon him, which in the Persian language is called tāvān (reparation/compensation). [346] The thought of salvation from the Fire must be achieved both through worship (‘ibādah) and through other good deeds. When Imam Ali عليه السلام dedicated properties (as waqf - endowment) in the way of Allah, he would write in his endowment deed: I have dedicated these properties so that by this means I may be safe from the Fire of Hell, and the Fire of Hell may also be far from me. [347]

346) Al-Tahqīq fī Kalimāt al-Qur’ān. 347) Sāfī, Vol. 2, p. 104.

Messages

1- Even if you are one who keeps vigil at night, do not become arrogant. «والّذین یقولون ...»

2- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma‘ād) is among the characteristics of Allah’s special servants. «ربّنا اصرف عنا عذاب جهنّم»

3- Allah’s special servants fear the Fire of Hell more than they covet Paradise. «یبیّتون... یقولون ربّنا اصرف عنا عذاب جهنّم»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَنفَقُوا۟ لَمْ يُسْرِفُوا۟ وَلَمْ يَقْتُرُوا۟ وَكَانَ بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ قَوَامًا (٦۷)
Those who are neither wasteful nor tightfisted when spending, but balanced between these [two extremes]. (67)

Key Points

The word qawām (قَوام) means the middle ground (or moderation), and the word qiwām (قِوام) means the means of sustenance (or establishment/upholding). Imam Reza عليه السلام considered the amount of moderate expenditure and spending (in charity) to be the well-known amount mentioned in Surah Al-Baqarah: «على الموسع قدره و على المقتر قدره متاعا بالمعروف» (348) meaning that the wealthy should give according to his capacity and the poor according to his ability, in accordance with acceptable custom and his status. (349) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Giving charity in the way of falsehood is extravagance (isrāf), even if it is little, and stinginess in the way of truth is restriction (iqtār). (350) In another place in the Holy Quran, we read: «لا تجعل یدك مغلولةً الى عنقك و لا تبسطها كل البسط» (351) Do not chain your hand to your neck (being stingy), nor open it completely (being extravagant and wasteful). Imam Sadiq عليه السلام picked up some pebbles from the ground, closed His fist, and said: This is iqtār (strictness and stinginess). Then He picked up another handful and opened His hand completely so that all the pebbles fell to the ground, and then He said: This is isrāf (extravagance). The third time, He picked up another handful and opened His hand slightly, so that some of the pebbles fell and some remained in His hand. Then He said: This is qawām, moderation, and the middle ground. (352)

348) Baqarah, 236. 349) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 56. 350) Tafsir Namūneh. 351) Isrā’, 29. 352) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā’iq.

Messages

1- Spending (in charity) is a definite duty and matter for the Servants of the Most Compassionate (‘Ibād al-Raḥmān). «اذا انفقوا...» (In Arabic grammar, idhā (اِذا) indicates realization/occurrence, while law (لَو) indicates non-occurrence.)

2- Night prayer, fear of Hell, and awe of Allah must be accompanied by attending to the deprived members of society. «یبیتون لربّهم - انفقوا»

3- Man is an owner, but he has limitations in spending. «لم یسرفوا»

4- Extravagance (isrāf) is not permissible, even in charity. «اذا انفقوا لم یسرفوا»

5- The special servants of Allah are far removed from stinginess. «لم یقتروا»

6- The moderate and middle nation must have balanced programs. «و كان بین ذالك قواما»

7- Moderation in worship and spending is a virtue. «قَواما»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَدْعُونَ مَعَ ٱللَّهِ إِلَـٰهًا ءَاخَرَ وَلَا يَقْتُلُونَ ٱلنَّفْسَ ٱلَّتِى حَرَّمَ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ وَلَا يَزْنُونَ ۚ وَمَن يَفْعَلْ ذَٰلِكَ يَلْقَ أَثَامًا (٦۸)
Those who do not invoke another deity besides Allah, and do not kill a soul [whose life] Allah has made inviolable, except with due cause, and do not commit fornication. (Whoever does that shall encounter its retribution, (68)

Key Points

Ithm (إثم) is sin, and athām (آثام) is the punishment for sin. In these verses, alongside the positive deeds of Allah's special servants, the negative deeds they avoid are also mentioned. In this verse, shirk (polytheism), murder, and zinā (illegal sexual intercourse/adultery) are discussed, which are counted among the greatest sins in narrations. [353] Killing a soul is among the major sins (gunāhān-e kabīrah), but the phrase "الاّ بالحقّ" (except by right) indicates that killing is necessary and rightful in certain cases, including: 1- Retaliation (Qisas) against the murderer. 2- Illegal sexual intercourse with close relatives (incest). 3- Illegal sexual intercourse committed by a disbeliever with a Muslim woman. 4- An adulterer/fornicator who forces a woman (rape). 5- Adultery committed by a married person (Zina al-Muhsan). 6- One who falsely claims prophethood. 7- Insulting, reviling, or cursing the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام. 8- A Muslim sorcerer/magician. 9- The corruptor (Mufsid) and the aggressor (Muhārib). 10- The apostate (Murtadd). 11- One who rebels against the just Imam of the time. 12- A polytheist (Mushrik) who is an aggressor/combatant (Muhārib). [354]

353) Tafsir Kanz al-Daqā'iq and Namūneh. 354) Sīmā-ye ‘Ibād al-Rahmān dar Qur’ān, Karimi Jahromi.

Messages

1- The most dangerous instincts that compel a person toward sin are the instincts of anger (wrath) and lust (desire), and a believer must control his instincts. "لا یقتلون - لا یزنون"

2- Allah is pleased with the killing of certain individuals in some cases. "الا بالحق" (except by right). However, He is never pleased, under any circumstances, that a person should be tainted by polytheism (shirk) or illegal sexual intercourse (zinā). "لا یزنون"

3- The Quranic command regarding the avoidance of polytheism, illegal sexual intercourse, and killing is not merely an admonition; it is a law whose violation carries a severe punishment. "ومن یفعل ذلك یلق اثاما" (And whoever does that will meet the punishment).

4- Divine punishments are not subject to exception; whoever violates the law will be punished. "و من یفعل ذلك یلق اثاما" (And whoever does that will meet the punishment).

Ayahs

يُضَـٰعَفْ لَهُ ٱلْعَذَابُ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ وَيَخْلُدْ فِيهِۦ مُهَانًا (٦۹)
the punishment being doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection. In it he will abide in humiliation forever, (69)
إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَءَامَنَ وَعَمِلَ عَمَلًا صَـٰلِحًا فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُبَدِّلُ ٱللَّهُ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ حَسَنَـٰتٍ ۗ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًا رَّحِيمًا (۷٠)
except those who repent, attain faith, and act righteously. For such, Allah will replace their misdeeds with good deeds, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful. (70)

Key Points

The multiplication of the punishment for the criminals, mentioned in the phrase «Yudha'af» (is multiplied), does not contradict Divine Justice, because the multiplied punishment occurs when the sin has multiple, ominous consequences. For example, the adulterer (or fornicator) both commits a sin and compels another person to sin, and sometimes, this sin results in the birth of an illegitimate child who possesses negative tendencies. In the case of murder (killing a soul), the killer slays one person, but leaves others grieving, without a guardian, orphaned, and makes society insecure. Each of these aspects is punishable individually, just as if someone establishes a bad tradition (sunnah) in society, throughout history, whoever acts upon that tradition will have a sin recorded for its founder.

Since, by the consensus of all scholars and exegetes, adultery (or fornication) alone does not cause eternity in Hellfire, the issue of khulood (eternity) in Hellfire is therefore only for polytheists who are also adulterers. Yes, the true or false belief, and the worldview of disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk), are influential in determining punishments.

Imam Reza عليه السلام said: It was said to the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: So-and-so is a wretched person because he has committed many sins. The Prophet said: It is never as you say; rather, he was saved, achieved a good end, and his sins were erased and transformed into good deeds (hasanat). This was because one day he was passing by a road and saw a believer whose private parts were exposed, and the believer himself was unaware (it seems he was asleep). This person covered his body and did not inform him so that he would not be embarrassed. When that believer realized what happened, he prayed for him, saying: May Allah grant you abundant reward, make your Hereafter accompanied by goodness, and not be strict or contentious with you in the reckoning. Allah answered the prayer of that believer concerning that person, and he achieved a good end. When this good news from the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله reached that sinner, he repented from all his sins and became devoted to the obedience of Allah. A week later, when a group of enemies of Islam attacked Medina, the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله ordered a defense. That man also prepared for defense along with other Muslims, and in the conflict with the enemies, he attained martyrdom, and his end concluded with goodness and felicity. [355]

There are factors that multiply the punishment. Some of these factors are as follows:

1- Sometimes having a social status doubles the punishment for the criminal. Allah says to the wives of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله: «مَن یَأت منكُنّ بِفاحشَة مُبیّنَة یُضاعف لها العَذاب» [356] (Whoever among you, O wives of the Prophet, commits a clear immorality, the punishment shall be doubled for her.)

2- Sometimes the time doubles the sin, such as sin committed on Friday, as it is mentioned in narrations that a good or evil deed on Friday is weighed double. [357]

3- Sometimes the sin is a key sin, meaning that besides the person's own deviation, it also deviates others. «الّذین یَصدّون عن سبیل اللّه و یَبغونها عِوجاً... یُضاعف لهم العذاب» [358]

355) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 155. 356) Al-Ahzab, 30. 357) Bihar, Vol. 89, p. 283. 358) Hud, 19-20.

Messages

1- The punishments of the Day of Judgment are both quantitative («yakhluud» - they abide) and qualitative («muhānan» - disgraced).

2- Islam has no dead ends. The sinner also has a path back. «Illā man tāba» (Except for those who repent).

3- When committing a sin, faith is lost, and upon repentance, it returns. «Illā man tāba wa āmana» (Except for those who repent and believe).

4- Repentance is a comprehensive revolution, not a superficial or verbal action; righteous deed is the sign of true repentance. «Tāba wa āmana wa ‘amila» (Repented, believed, and acted).

5- True repentance and faith change a person's deeds and rectify his undesirable behavior. «Wa ‘amila ‘amalan ṣāliḥan» (And performed a righteous deed).

6- Just as Allah creates flowers from soil and manure in the creation of existence, He transforms the evil deeds (sayyi’āt) of humans into good deeds (ḥasanāt). «Yubaddilu Allāhu sayyi’ātihim ḥasanāt» (Allah will change their evil deeds into good deeds).

Ayah

وَمَن تَابَ وَعَمِلَ صَـٰلِحًا فَإِنَّهُۥ يَتُوبُ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَتَابًا (۷۱)
And whoever repents and acts righteously indeed turns to Allah with due penitence). (71)

Messages

1- True repentance is that which is accompanied by righteous deeds. «تاب و عمل صالحا»

2- The repenter is Allah's guest. «یتوب الى اللّه»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَشْهَدُونَ ٱلزُّورَ وَإِذَا مَرُّوا۟ بِٱللَّغْوِ مَرُّوا۟ كِرَامًا (۷۲)
Those who do not give false testimony, and when they come upon frivolity, pass by with dignity. (72)

Key Points

The word shahida (شَهِد) has two meanings: one is being present, and the other is reporting and testifying. In this Ayah, both meanings can be utilized. Meaning, they neither attend evil gatherings nor testify to falsehood. The word zūr (زور) means a vain act presented in the guise of truth, and in the tafāsīr (commentaries), it has been interpreted as false testimony, lying, and singing (ghinā). In a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, the meaning of zūr in "لایشهدون الزور" (they do not witness falsehood) is considered to be ghinā (singing/music). [359] We read in the commentary Majma‘ al-Bayān: Passing with dignity is when, if they are forced to mention the name of something ugly, they refer to it euphemistically. Just as we read in a narration from Imam Baqir عليه السلام: When they wish to mention the name of the private parts (awrah), they refer to it euphemistically. Of course, this is one instance of dignified behavior. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Allah made it obligatory upon the ear to avoid hearing everything that is forbidden (harām), then He recited this Ayah: «واذا مرّوا باللغو مرّوا كراما» [360]

359) Kāfī, Vol. 6, p. 433. 360) Tafsir Nūr al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Not only is committing sin forbidden (harām), but participating in a sinful gathering and witnessing sin is also prohibited. «لا یشهدون الزور»

2- Not every type of awareness, knowledge, or presence at a scene is valuable. «لا یشهدون الزور» (Sometimes isolation and withdrawal from society is valuable.)

3- Not attending a sinful gathering and showing indifference toward sinners is a form of Nahy ‘anil-Munkar (forbidding the wrong). «لایشهدون... مرّوا كراما»

4- Participation in corrupt gatherings affects even the true servants of Allah. «لا یشهدون»

5- Every kind of futility (laghw) in speech, action, and desire is prohibited. «مرّوا باللغو» (The servants of Allah have a rational and beneficial goal and do not spend their lives on vain matters.)

6- In the method of Nahy ‘anil-Munkar, harshness is not always necessary. «لا یشهدون... مرّوا»

7- We must not allow evil thoughts to settle in our souls. «مرّوا كراما»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَا ذُكِّرُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ رَبِّهِمْ لَمْ يَخِرُّوا۟ عَلَيْهَا صُمًّا وَعُمْيَانًا (۷۳)
Those who, when reminded of the signs of their Lord, do not turn a deaf ear and a blind eye to them. (73)
وَٱلَّذِينَ يَقُولُونَ رَبَّنَا هَبْ لَنَا مِنْ أَزْوَٰجِنَا وَذُرِّيَّـٰتِنَا قُرَّةَ أَعْيُنٍ وَٱجْعَلْنَا لِلْمُتَّقِينَ إِمَامًا (۷٤)
And those who say, ‘Our Lord! Give us joy and comfort in our spouses and offspring, and make us imams of the Godwary.’ (74)

Key Points

Faith must be based on awareness and insight. In the Quran, we read: «و یَتفكّرون فى خَلق السموات والارض ربّنا ما خَلَقت هذا باطلاً» [361] Meaning, wise believers first reflect upon the creation of the heavens and the earth, and then they acknowledge: O Lord! You have not created these in vain. In this Ayah too, He says: Believers do not worship blindly.

361) Al ‘Imran, 191.

Messages

1- Devotion and insight are among the characteristics of the Servants of the Most Merciful (عباد الرّحمن). «اذا ذكّروا... لم یخرّواعلیهاصُماً وعمیاناً» The Servants of the Most Merciful approach religion consciously.

2- Man is responsible toward his spouse, children, and descendants, and he must strive and pray for their good outcome. «ربّنا هب لنا من ازواجنا...»

3- In education and upbringing, besides knowledge and effort, one must also seek help from Allah. «ربّنا هب لنا...»

4- A righteous child is the delight of the eye. «قرّة اعین»

5- Forming a family, having a spouse and children, and praying to attain them, are matters of importance in Islam. «ربّنا هب لنا...»

6- Taking charge of the management of society is the aspiration of the Servants of the Most Merciful, because undertaking the Imamat (leadership) and guidance of the pious (al-Muttaqin) is valuable. «واجعلنا للمتّقین اماما»

7- Do not settle for the minimum in prayer; be ambitious. «واجعلنا للمتّقین اماما» (O Allah, make us the leader of the pious in reaching perfection, not merely one of the pious.)

8- The Servants of Allah have unity of purpose and unity of word. «اماماً» (Imam, singular) was used, and He did not say: «ائمّةً» (Imams, plural). (It is possible that the word «امام» instead of «ائمّة» is a symbol that in an Islamic society, the leader must be one person.)

Ayahs

أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ يُجْزَوْنَ ٱلْغُرْفَةَ بِمَا صَبَرُوا۟ وَيُلَقَّوْنَ فِيهَا تَحِيَّةً وَسَلَـٰمًا (۷٥)
Those shall be rewarded with sublime abodes for their patience and steadfastness, and they shall be met there with greetings and ‘Peace,’ (75)
خَـٰلِدِينَ فِيهَا ۚ حَسُنَتْ مُسْتَقَرًّا وَمُقَامًا (۷٦)
to abide in them [forever], an excellent station and abode. (76)

Key Points

«غُرفة» (Ghurfah) refers to a room that is elevated above the ground level and has a view.

Messages

1- Paradise is granted for a price, not for an excuse. «یُجزون... بما صبروا»

2- Becoming a servant of Allah requires patience and perseverance. «بما صبروا»

Ayah

قُلْ مَا يَعْبَؤُا۟ بِكُمْ رَبِّى لَوْلَا دُعَآؤُكُمْ ۖ فَقَدْ كَذَّبْتُمْ فَسَوْفَ يَكُونُ لِزَامًۢا (۷۷)
Say, ‘Were it not for the sake of summoning you [to faith], what store my Lord would have set by you? But you impugned [me and my summons], so it will soon follow as a result.’ (77)

Key Points

The word ‘aba’ (عَبأ) means weight and significance. The phrase “mā ya‘ba’u bikum Rabbī” (ما یعبأ بكم ربّى) means that Allah assigns no weight or value to you, except in the shadow of your supplication (du‘ā) and worship.

The word “du‘ā’ukum” (دعائكم) has been interpreted in two ways:

A: Your supplication to Allah, where these very cries, pleas, and prayers are the cause of Allah’s favor upon you. As we read in a hadith: Whoever is devoted to supplication will not perish. [362]

Then Allah complains about the opposing group who are not people of supplication, saying that you denied the Truth, and instead of praying, you turned to idols, desires, and tyrants (ṭāghūts), and you denied the Truth, so you shall see your punishment.

B: Allah’s invitation to people, because the Divine tradition is to invite people to accept the Truth and to complete the argument (itmām al-ḥujjah) against them. If this invitation were not present; “lawlā du‘ā’ukum” (لولا دعائكم), people would have no value. What makes them superior and valuable beings is the acceptance of Allah’s invitation, but you did not accept the Divine invitation and you denied it, so there is no hope of good in you, and you shall receive the recompense for your deeds.

In one place, Allah says: «و ما خلقتُ الجنّ و الانس الاّ لِیعبدون» [363] (I created mankind for worship), and in this āyah, He says: If man’s supplication were not present, he would have no value. Therefore, supplication is the spirit and core of worship. As we read in a narration: «الدعاء مُخّ العبادة» [364] (Supplication is the marrow/essence of worship).

362) Kāfī, Vol. 4, p. 228. 363) Adh-Dhāriyāt, 56. 364) Ghurar al-Ḥikam.

Messages

1- Denying religion causes the downfall of human value. «قل ما یعبؤا بكم... فقد كذّبتم»

2- Supplication (du‘ā) is the means of acquiring value. «ما یعبؤا بكم... لولا دعاؤكم»

3- Man, devoid of guidance and worship, is insignificant. «ما یعبؤا بكم... لولا دعائكم»

4- Inviting to the Truth is among the affairs of Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ربّى لولا دعائكم»

5- The axis of existence is spirituality. «ما یعبؤا بكم... لولا دعائكم» because existence was created for man, and man was created to accept the Truth and worship Allah.

6- Man, devoid of guidance and worship, has in reality denied the Prophets and their commands. «لولا دعائكم فقد كذّبتم»

Portrait of Surah Ash-Shu‘arā’

This Surah has 227 āyāt, revealed in Mecca, and after Surah Al-Baqarah, it contains the highest number of verses. This Surah is named Ash-Shu‘arā’ (The Poets) because of its praise for believing poets and its condemnation of frivolous poets, which appears in its last four āyāt. Its other name is Ṭā Sīn Mīm (طسم).

The stories of Prophets such as Moses, Abraham, Noah, Lot, Salih, Hud, and Shu‘ayb علیهم السلام, and the stubborn confrontations of the people with them, are recounted in this Surah. At the end of every story, it states: «اِنّ فى ذلك لآیة و ما كان اكثرهم مؤمنین و اِنّ ربّك لهو العزیز الحكیم» This āyah is repeated eight times to console the Prophet of Islam and tell him not to worry about the obstinacy of the people of Mecca, because all the Prophets suffered from such people.

Indeed, history is the best means for strengthening the morale of the believers, increasing insight, broadening tolerance, and providing foresight against the threats of enemies. Therefore, the leaders of society must be aware of the history of society.
